machine.name: D3D v18.1
machine.displayName: D3D v18.1 - Full FreeCAD Assembly: Frame, Axes, Z-Axis, Extruder, Heated Bed, and Cable Management
machine.description: Complete D3D v18.1 assembly covering all 61 FreeCAD-sourced parts plus 32 procedural parts. Stages: frame fabrication and cube joining, Y/X axis composite units and their internal decomposition (motors, idlers, rods, carriages, brackets), X-axis drive, Z-axis vertical motion columns (front and back), three extruder stages, heated bed mechanical stack with bed platform (raisers, guide rods, Y-bed rods), and cable management routing.
learningObjectives: Recognize the stock dimensions used to fabricate the D3D frame sides
Place all 24 flat bars in their real 14 inch square side geometry
Preserve the 1 inch overlap that converts 13 inch bar stock into a 14 inch outer square
Check each side for squareness before heat is introduced
Clamp and tack-weld each verified side while it remains pinned flat
Place the finished frame-side panels into their correct cube positions
Check, hold, tack, seam-weld, clean up, and recheck the stacked cube before motion hardware is added
Mount the first Y-left and Y-right axis units to the accepted frame
Fit the first X-axis module tightly between the completed Y-axis pair
Lock, tension, and check the first X-axis module without visible gap or binding
Build the first Titan Aero nozzle, fan, and sensor module before later carriage integration
Assemble the carriage-side Titan Aero bracket, top plate, and simplified carriage stack before final printer-wide extruder mounting is introduced
Mount the completed extruder carriage unit onto the X-axis carriage interface and close the half-carriage clamp before later heated-bed and wiring modules are authored
Stage the first mechanical heated-bed body and retainer pair before later heater wiring and control integration are introduced
prerequisiteNotes: The six panel work zones are a tighter raised teaching layout so small real-size stock remains readable and selectable while preserving exact panel geometry.
This package now covers frame fabrication, cube joining, and a first axes stage.
Axes Stage 01 uses source-backed procedure order and mounting relationships, but the axis module shapes remain schematic placeholders until exact v18.1 axis dimensions are extracted.
Extruder Stage 01 locks the Titan Aero path and stops at the nozzle / fan / sensor module before the unresolved v18.1 carriage-mount variant is authored.
Extruder Stage 02 assembles the carriage-side bracket, top plate, and simplified carriage stack only. Final printer-wide mounted orientation, spacer usage, and wiring remain deferred.
Extruder Stage 03 mounts the completed carriage unit onto the X-axis carriage interface and closes the half-carriage clamp. The spacer and finer closure-fastener story remain deferred.
Heated Bed Stage 01 is mechanical-only: the bed body and snapbuckle pair are authored now, while wirelock, heater wiring, and later control/electronics details remain deferred.

assemblies.name: D3D Frame
assemblies.description: Complete set of six D3D frame sides authored as source-backed layup, clamp-up, tack-welded panels, stacked cube targets, bracket-based cube joining with staged bolt tightening, post-bracket square verification, and frame acceptance before motion hardware.
assemblies.learningFocus: Repeatable frame-side geometry, square verification, stable hold-down, correct spatial stacking of the finished frame sides into the cube, bracket-based cube joining with staged bolt tightening, square verification between stages, and the acceptance logic used before motion hardware is added.

assemblies.name: Y-Left Axis Bench Build
assemblies.description: Assembles the Y-left axis module from individual parts on the workbench: seats linear bearings in the carriage halves, builds the idler with flanged bearing, preps the motor holder with pulley and belt, threads all three units onto the guide rods, routes and pegs the belt, then glues the endstop to the motor holder. Produces a complete, labeled Y-left axis module ready for frame mounting.
assemblies.learningFocus: Carriage bearing fit and belt-hole alignment, idler bearing and rod-hole alignment, motor pulley press-fit and belt channel, rod threading order (idler → carriage → motor holder), belt routing and peg locking, endstop gluing, and full bench QC before frame mounting.

assemblies.name: Y-Right Axis Bench Build
assemblies.description: Assembles the Y-right axis module from individual parts using the same procedure as Y-left. Y-right and Z-Front share the same plastic piece type — build instructions are identical. Note the slight orientation differences in motor cable direction and long-bolt position. Produces a labeled Y-right axis module ready for frame mounting.
assemblies.learningFocus: Same bench-build sequence as Y-left: carriage bearing shake and rod-slide tests, idler rod-hole alignment, motor pulley pop-check, rod threading order, belt routing and first peg locked. Note: long M6x30 bolt is in bottom-left of motor half-piece (not top), and motor cable plug faces the belt drive hole.

assemblies.name: X-Axis Bench Build
assemblies.description: Assembles the X-axis module from individual parts: seats bearings in the carriage, builds the idler with 5 M6x18 screws and 2 half-bearings, preps the motor holder with belt channel, threads onto guide rods, routes and pegs the belt, and glues the X-axis endstop to the motor holder. Produces a labeled X-axis module ready for fitting between the Y carriages.
assemblies.learningFocus: X-axis carriage uses same bearing fit tests as Y. X-axis idler uses 5 M6x18 screws and 2 half-bearings with flanges out. Motor holder: two nuts in half-piece, belt toothed-side inward, 4 M3x25 motor screws + 3 M6x18 bolts + 1 M6x30 bolt. Second belt peg left loose — tensioned during frame fitting, not at bench.

assemblies.name: Z-Front Axis Bench Build
assemblies.description: Assembles the Z-front axis module from individual parts. Z-Front uses the same plastic piece type as Y-Right. No endstop is attached to Z axes. The long M6x30 frame-mount bolt on the idler is left loose at bench build. Produces a complete Z-front axis module ready for mounting on the front frame face.
assemblies.learningFocus: Z-Front uses the same build procedure as Y-Right (same plastic piece type). No endstop. The spacer pieces go between the carriage and the half-carriage during rod threading. The second belt peg is left loose — it is tensioned after the axis is mounted and sized to the front frame face.

assemblies.name: Z-Back Axis Bench Build
assemblies.description: Assembles the Z-back axis module from individual parts. Z-Back uses the same plastic piece type as Y-Left. No endstop is attached to Z axes. Produces a complete Z-back axis module ready for mounting on the back frame face directly behind Z-front.
assemblies.learningFocus: Z-Back uses the same build procedure as Y-Left (same plastic piece type). No endstop. During frame mounting, Z-back must be aligned directly behind Z-front before motor-piece bolts are locked — verify both carriages are at equal height.

assemblies.name: D3D Extruder Stage 01
assemblies.description: Stages the Titan Aero core, attaches the nozzle assembly, builds the first fan and sensor-side attachments, inserts the 8 mm sensor, and verifies clearance before later carriage integration.
assemblies.learningFocus: Titan Aero module decomposition, nozzle-side extruder sequencing, first fan and sensor-side attachments, and a conservative service-clearance check before the unresolved carriage-mount variant is introduced.

assemblies.name: D3D Extruder Stage 02
assemblies.description: Stages the simplified carriage, assembles the first carriage-side Titan Aero bracket and top-plate stack, mounts the completed nozzle module onto that carriage-side stack, and checks service clearance before later printer-wide integration.
assemblies.learningFocus: Carriage-side extruder mount-stack assembly, bracket and top-plate fastening order, rigid nozzle-module mounting, and service-clearance checks before later printer-wide extruder integration.

assemblies.name: D3D Extruder Stage 03
assemblies.description: Stages the X-axis carriage-side interface, mounts the completed extruder carriage unit onto it, closes the half-carriage clamp, and checks X-axis travel clearance before later heated-bed and wiring work.
assemblies.learningFocus: Printer-side extruder integration, carriage-side closure with the half carriage, and first-pass X-travel/nozzle-clearance verification.

assemblies.name: D3D Heated Bed Stage 01
assemblies.description: Stages the first mechanical heated-bed body into the printer, places the snapbuckle retainer pair, and checks first bed-envelope / nozzle clearance before heater wiring and control work are introduced.
assemblies.learningFocus: Mechanical bed-body placement, first retainer placement, and first bed-envelope / nozzle-clearance review before any heated-bed wiring is authored.

assemblies.name: D3D Axes Frame Mount
assemblies.description: Mounts all five completed axis modules to the welded frame in sequence: Y-left and Y-right directly to frame side faces, X-axis fitted and tensioned between the Y carriages, then Z-front and Z-back to the front and back frame faces. All bench-built axis modules must be complete before this assembly begins.
assemblies.learningFocus: Y-left idler orientation through left-face hole, all 8 motor-holder bolts tightened before mounting (inaccessible after), Y-right mirroring Y-left with motors at back, five-step X-axis fitting procedure between Y carriages, Z-front idler at front-face bottom hole offset right for print area, Z-back aligned directly behind Z-front.

assemblies.name: D3D Heated Bed Stage 02 (Bed Platform)
assemblies.description: Bed platform assembly: six standoff raisers, two bed guide rods, and three Y-axis bed travel rods. The platform rides on the Y carriages and supports the heated bed at the correct height.

assemblies.name: D3D Cable Management
assemblies.description: Cable chain routing for X-axis and Y-axis wiring. Protects wiring from motion-induced wear and keeps cables organized during printer operation.

assemblies.name: D3D Electronics and Wiring
assemblies.description: Control panel mounting, RAMPS wiring, motor connectors, endstops, and extruder electronics.
assemblies.learningFocus: Control panel mounting, RAMPS board installation, PSU power wiring with polarity validation, stepper motor connections, axis endstop wiring, and extruder heater/thermistor/fan connections.

subassemblies.name: Bottom Frame Side
subassemblies.description: Lays out, checks, clamps, and tack-welds the bottom frame side as one 14 inch square panel.
subassemblies.milestoneMessage: Bottom Frame Side laid out, held, and tack-welded.

subassemblies.name: Top Frame Side
subassemblies.description: Lays out, checks, clamps, and tack-welds the top frame side as one 14 inch square panel.
subassemblies.milestoneMessage: Top Frame Side laid out, held, and tack-welded.

subassemblies.name: Left Frame Side
subassemblies.description: Lays out, checks, clamps, and tack-welds the left frame side as one 14 inch square panel.
subassemblies.milestoneMessage: Left Frame Side laid out, held, and tack-welded.

subassemblies.name: Right Frame Side
subassemblies.description: Lays out, checks, clamps, and tack-welds the right frame side as one 14 inch square panel.
subassemblies.milestoneMessage: Right Frame Side laid out, held, and tack-welded.

subassemblies.name: Front Frame Side
subassemblies.description: Lays out, checks, clamps, and tack-welds the front frame side as one 14 inch square panel.
subassemblies.milestoneMessage: Front Frame Side laid out, held, and tack-welded.

subassemblies.name: Rear Frame Side
subassemblies.description: Lays out, checks, clamps, and tack-welds the rear frame side as one 14 inch square panel.
subassemblies.milestoneMessage: Rear Frame Side laid out, held, and tack-welded.

subassemblies.name: Frame Cube Joining
subassemblies.description: Uses the six completed frame-side panels as rigid learner-placeable units, positions them into the open printer cube, checks the stacked frame, attaches 8 printed PLA corner brackets with M6 bolts in a staged finger-tight then final-tighten sequence, verifies squareness between stages, and accepts the frame before later subsystems are mounted.
subassemblies.milestoneMessage: The printer cube is stacked, bracketed at all eight corners, bolt-tightened, verified for square, and accepted for later motion hardware.

subassemblies.name: Y-Left Axis Frame Mount
subassemblies.description: Mounts the completed bench-built Y-left axis unit onto the left face of the welded frame and verifies smooth travel.
subassemblies.milestoneMessage: Y-left axis mounted and motion verified.

subassemblies.name: Y-Right Axis Frame Mount
subassemblies.description: Mounts the completed bench-built Y-right axis unit onto the right face of the welded frame and confirms the Y-axis pair establishes the X-axis span.
subassemblies.milestoneMessage: Y-right axis mounted. Both Y axes are parallel with motors at back.

subassemblies.name: X-Axis Frame Fit
subassemblies.description: Fits the completed bench-built X-axis module between the Y carriages using the five-step source-backed procedure: tighten idler screws, anchor idler to Y-right, loosen motor holder, span to Y-left, lock and tension.
subassemblies.milestoneMessage: X-axis fitted tightly between Y axes, belt tensioned, and travel checked.

subassemblies.name: Titan Aero Core
subassemblies.description: Stages the Titan Aero core and adds the nozzle assembly as the base of the first extruder module slice.
subassemblies.milestoneMessage: Titan Aero core and nozzle assembly staged.

subassemblies.name: Extruder Sensor and Fan Attachment
subassemblies.description: Adds the first print-cooling blower, sensor holder, and 8 mm sensor around the staged Titan Aero core.
subassemblies.milestoneMessage: First fan and sensor-side hardware added to the nozzle module.

subassemblies.name: Extruder Nozzle Module
subassemblies.description: Owns the first Titan Aero nozzle, blower, sensor-holder, and 8 mm sensor sequence before later carriage integration.
subassemblies.milestoneMessage: Titan Aero nozzle module built and checked for first-pass clearance.

subassemblies.name: Extruder Carriage Mount Stack
subassemblies.description: Stages the simplified carriage and assembles the first carriage-side Titan Aero bracket and top-plate stack before the nozzle module is mounted.
subassemblies.milestoneMessage: Simplified carriage, bracket, and top plate assembled into the first carriage-side Titan Aero mount stack.

subassemblies.name: Extruder Carriage Unit
subassemblies.description: Combines the completed Titan Aero nozzle module with the carriage-side bracket and top-plate stack to create the first carriage-side extruder unit.
subassemblies.milestoneMessage: Titan Aero nozzle module mounted to the carriage-side bracket stack and checked for first-pass service clearance.

subassemblies.name: Extruder X-Axis Mount
subassemblies.description: Stages the printer-side X-axis carriage interface and the half-carriage clamp used to close the extruder around the rod pair.
subassemblies.milestoneMessage: The completed extruder carriage unit is mounted on the X-axis carriage side, closed with the half carriage, and checked for first-pass travel clearance.

subassemblies.name: Heated Bed Mechanical Stack
subassemblies.description: First-pass heated-bed mechanical stack made from the bed body and snapbuckle retainer pair.
subassemblies.milestoneMessage: The first mechanical heated-bed body and snapbuckle retainer pair are placed and checked for initial bed-envelope / nozzle clearance.

subassemblies.name: Y-Left Carriage Assembly
subassemblies.description: Seats four linear bearings in the carriage half-pieces with flanges facing out, aligns the rectangular belt holes (small ribbed hole vs large smooth hole), and bolts the halves together with M6x18 bolts at top and M6x30 bolts at bottom. Verify with shake test (bearings must not rattle) and rod-slide test (rod drops with slight resistance). Wrap bearings in electrical tape if too loose.
subassemblies.milestoneMessage: Y-left carriage assembled: bearings seated, belt holes aligned, halves bolted.

subassemblies.name: Y-Left Idler Assembly
subassemblies.description: Inserts M6x18 bolt through one idler half, seats two flanged bearings on the bolt with flanges facing out, aligns the two halves so rod holes line up, secures the bearing bolt fully, inserts M6x30 frame-mount bolt loosely (will attach to frame later), and tightens the remaining M6x18 bolt loosely.
subassemblies.milestoneMessage: Y-left idler assembled. Frame-mount bolt left loose for sizing at frame.

subassemblies.name: Y-Left Motor Holder Assembly
subassemblies.description: Places pulley on motor shaft with thin spacer, tightens first set screw, removes spacer, tightens second set screw until pop. Preps motor half-piece with one M6x30 bolt (bottom-left hole, poking up) and three nuts. Places belt toothed-side inward in belt channel of other half. Closes halves gently. Inserts motor with cable plug facing bottom-right. Tightens 4 M3x25 motor screws ¾ tight then 3 M6x18 bolts. Performs dangle test.
subassemblies.milestoneMessage: Y-left motor holder assembled and dangle-tested.

subassemblies.name: Y-Left Axis Rod Assembly
subassemblies.description: Inserts the two guide rods into the completed idler (rods flush with idler bottom), slides the carriage onto the rods (long bolt ends closer to idler), then pushes the motor holder onto the rods (motor facing the carriage bolt heads). Tightens the idler's two shorter bolts. Checks rods are still flush with idler bottom.
subassemblies.milestoneMessage: Y-left guide rods threaded through idler → carriage → motor holder with rods flush at idler.

subassemblies.name: Y-Left Belt Threading
subassemblies.description: Inserts one belt end through the larger carriage belt hole, routes it around the idler bearing (slight curve helps), pulls through the smaller carriage belt hole. Threads through first peg (foot facing away from axis center), inserts belt end ~¾ inch and pushes peg into ribbed hole. Places second peg loosely — it will be tightened after frame mounting when the full belt length is confirmed.
subassemblies.milestoneMessage: Y-left belt routed and first peg locked. Second peg loose for frame-side tensioning.

subassemblies.name: Y-Left Endstop Attachment
subassemblies.description: Slides endstop board into holder crevice with switch arm facing the arrowhead direction, bottom flush with holder bottom. Dabs super glue into top-left and bottom-left motor-piece holes facing the belt. Presses and holds the endstop holder against the motor piece. Adds reinforcing glue along the holder edges. Labels axis Y-Left with masking tape.
subassemblies.milestoneMessage: Y-left axis complete: endstop glued, QC passed (belt smooth, rods flush, bolt heads same side, belt toothed side inward), axis labeled.

subassemblies.name: Y-Right Carriage Assembly
subassemblies.description: Same procedure as Y-left carriage: seat four linear bearings with flanges out, align belt holes, bolt halves with M6x18 and M6x30 bolts. Run shake test and rod-slide test.
subassemblies.milestoneMessage: Y-right carriage assembled and bearing fit verified.

subassemblies.name: Y-Right Idler Assembly
subassemblies.description: Same procedure as Y-left idler. Orient idler with large rectangular hole to the left. Insert M6x30 screw into top hole so screw end faces you. Leave all frame-mount bolts loose.
subassemblies.milestoneMessage: Y-right idler assembled. Frame-mount bolt left loose.

subassemblies.name: Y-Right Motor Holder Assembly
subassemblies.description: Same procedure as Y-left motor holder. Note: M6x30 bolt goes in bottom-left hole (right side of motor half-piece has belt drive). Motor cable plug faces belt drive hole opposite the long M6x30 bolt.
subassemblies.milestoneMessage: Y-right motor holder assembled and dangle-tested.

subassemblies.name: Y-Right Axis Rod Assembly
subassemblies.description: Inserts rods into idler flush with bottom, slides carriage onto rods (long bolts closer to idler), pushes motor holder onto rods (motor facing carriage bolt heads). Tightens idler's two shorter bolts and re-checks rods flush.
subassemblies.milestoneMessage: Y-right guide rods threaded through all three units with rods flush at idler.

subassemblies.name: Y-Right Belt Threading
subassemblies.description: Routes belt through carriage large hole, around idler bearing via belt-catcher side (smaller hole with teeth), pulls through and threads onto first peg, locks peg. Second peg placed loosely. Label axis Y-Right.
subassemblies.milestoneMessage: Y-right axis complete: belt routed, first peg locked, axis labeled and QC checked.

subassemblies.name: X-Axis Carriage Assembly
subassemblies.description: Same bearing-in-carriage procedure as Y axes. Middle holes must fit together as pictured. M6x18 bolts at top, M6x30 bolts at bottom. Run shake test and rod-slide test.
subassemblies.milestoneMessage: X-axis carriage assembled and bearing fit verified.

subassemblies.name: X-Axis Idler Assembly
subassemblies.description: X-axis idler uses 5 M6x18 screws and 2 half-bearings (not full bearings). Place bearings with flanges out. Put nuts beside bearings in their slots — these nuts attach the X-axis to the Y-axis carriages later. Add 2 M6x18 bolts through the half-piece, close and tighten fully.
subassemblies.milestoneMessage: X-axis idler assembled with nuts in place for Y-carriage attachment.

subassemblies.name: X-Axis Motor Holder Assembly
subassemblies.description: Sets two nuts in motor half-piece. Places belt toothed-side inward in channel. Closes half-pieces aligned. Inserts 4 nuts (no bolts yet). Pulley on motor with set screws. Motor placed on top with cable plug pointing toward belt. 4 M3x25 screws then 3 M6x18 bolts and 1 M6x30 bolt — note long bolt position relative to belt hole.
subassemblies.milestoneMessage: X-axis motor holder assembled.

subassemblies.name: X-Axis Rod Assembly
subassemblies.description: Inserts rods into completed idler flush with bottom. Slides carriage onto rods. Pushes motor holder onto rods in correct orientation. Checks rods still flush at idler.
subassemblies.milestoneMessage: X-axis guide rods threaded. Carriage slides smoothly.

subassemblies.name: X-Axis Belt Threading
subassemblies.description: Routes belt from large carriage hole, around idler bearing (slight curve helps), through idler belt-catcher side, through first peg (foot away from axis center), locks first peg. Second peg placed loosely — tightened after fitting to Y axes.
subassemblies.milestoneMessage: X-axis belt routed and first peg locked. Second peg loose for Y-fitting tensioning.

subassemblies.name: X-Axis Endstop Attachment
subassemblies.description: Slides X-axis endstop into holder crevice. Super glues holder to top-left and bottom-left motor-piece holes. Adds reinforcing glue along edges. Labels axis X-Axis. Verifies X and Y axes will fit together before setting aside.
subassemblies.milestoneMessage: X-axis complete: endstop glued, QC passed, axis labeled and dry-fitted to Y axes.

subassemblies.name: Z-Front Carriage Assembly
subassemblies.description: Same carriage build as Y-Right (same piece type). Seat bearings, align belt holes, bolt halves. Run shake and rod-slide tests.
subassemblies.milestoneMessage: Z-front carriage assembled and bearing fit verified.

subassemblies.name: Z-Front Idler Assembly
subassemblies.description: Same idler build as Y-Right. Long M6x30 frame-mount bolt placed loosely — it will size to the front frame face bottom hole. No bearing in the middle of Z idler (Z uses the same idler piece as Y-Right).
subassemblies.milestoneMessage: Z-front idler assembled. Frame-mount bolt left loose.

subassemblies.name: Z-Front Motor Holder Assembly
subassemblies.description: Same motor holder build as Y-Right. Belt in channel, motor attached. All 8 motor-piece bolts will be tightened before frame mounting (they become inaccessible after).
subassemblies.milestoneMessage: Z-front motor holder assembled.

subassemblies.name: Z-Front Axis Rod Assembly
subassemblies.description: Threads rods through idler, carriage (with spacers z2_spacer and z2_spacer_2 between carriage and half-carriage), and motor holder. Checks rods flush at idler.
subassemblies.milestoneMessage: Z-front guide rods threaded with spacers in place.

subassemblies.name: Z-Front Belt Threading
subassemblies.description: Routes belt, locks first peg, places second peg loosely. No endstop for Z axes. Label axis Z-Front.
subassemblies.milestoneMessage: Z-front axis complete: belt routed, first peg locked, axis labeled and QC checked.

subassemblies.name: Z-Back Carriage Assembly
subassemblies.description: Same carriage build as Y-Left (same piece type). Seat bearings, align belt holes, bolt halves. Run shake and rod-slide tests.
subassemblies.milestoneMessage: Z-back carriage assembled and bearing fit verified.

subassemblies.name: Z-Back Idler Assembly
subassemblies.description: Same idler build as Y-Left. Long M6x30 frame-mount bolt placed loosely. The Z-back idler mounts on the back face bottom hole directly behind Z-front.
subassemblies.milestoneMessage: Z-back idler assembled. Frame-mount bolt left loose.

subassemblies.name: Z-Back Motor Holder Assembly
subassemblies.description: Same motor holder build as Y-Left. All 8 motor-piece bolts will be pre-tightened at bench — they become inaccessible after frame mounting.
subassemblies.milestoneMessage: Z-back motor holder assembled.

subassemblies.name: Z-Back Axis Rod Assembly
subassemblies.description: Threads rods through idler, carriage (with spacers z1_spacer_1 and z1_spacer_002), and motor holder. Checks rods flush at idler.
subassemblies.milestoneMessage: Z-back guide rods threaded with spacers in place.

subassemblies.name: Z-Back Belt Threading
subassemblies.description: Routes belt, locks first peg, places second peg loosely. No endstop for Z axes. Label axis Z-Back.
subassemblies.milestoneMessage: Z-back axis complete: belt routed, first peg locked, axis labeled and QC checked.

subassemblies.name: Z-Front Axis Frame Mount
subassemblies.description: Mounts the completed bench-built Z-front axis module to the front frame face. Idler attaches at bottom hole offset right for print area, motor holder at top hole. Belt tensioned after mounting.
subassemblies.milestoneMessage: Z-front axis mounted, sized, belt tensioned and checked.

subassemblies.name: Z-Back Axis Frame Mount
subassemblies.description: Mounts the completed bench-built Z-back axis module to the back frame face directly behind Z-front. Both Z carriages must be at the same height before locking.
subassemblies.milestoneMessage: Z-back axis mounted, aligned with Z-front, belt tensioned and checked.

subassemblies.name: Heated Bed Platform
subassemblies.description: Bed standoff raisers and guide rods that support the heated bed above the Z carriages.

subassemblies.name: Y-Axis Bed Guide Rods
subassemblies.description: Guide rods for Y-axis bed travel, connecting front and back Z carriages.

subassemblies.name: Cable Routing
subassemblies.description: Cable chain assemblies for X-axis and Y-axis wiring routes.

subassemblies.name: Electronics and Wiring
subassemblies.description: Control panel, RAMPS board, PSU wiring, motor connectors, endstops, and extruder electronics.

subassemblies.name: Y Left Complete Axis Unit
subassemblies.description: The fully assembled y left axis unit, ready for staging. Contains all bench-built components: carriage, idler, motor holder, rods, and belt.

subassemblies.name: Y Right Complete Axis Unit
subassemblies.description: The fully assembled y right axis unit, ready for staging. Contains all bench-built components: carriage, idler, motor holder, rods, and belt.

subassemblies.name: X Axis Complete Axis Unit
subassemblies.description: The fully assembled x axis axis unit, ready for staging. Contains all bench-built components: carriage, idler, motor holder, rods, and belt.

subassemblies.name: Z Front Complete Axis Unit
subassemblies.description: The fully assembled z front axis unit, ready for staging. Contains all bench-built components: carriage, idler, motor holder, rods, and belt.

subassemblies.name: Z Back Complete Axis Unit
subassemblies.description: The fully assembled z back axis unit, ready for staging. Contains all bench-built components: carriage, idler, motor holder, rods, and belt.

tools.name: Framing Square
tools.purpose: Confirms that each D3D frame side stays square before clamping and tack welding.
tools.usageNotes: Seat both legs of the square against adjacent flat bars before any tack weld is placed.
tools.defaultOrientationHint: Hold the inside corner of the square against the bar intersection.
tools.safetyNotes: Keep the work surface clear so the square sits flat against both bars.

tools.name: Locking Clamp
tools.purpose: Pins a verified frame side flat so the overlapped bars cannot shift while tack welds are added.
tools.usageNotes: Clamp near the panel center after the square check passes, then keep the bars flat while you tack opposite corners.
tools.safetyNotes: Keep fingers clear of the jaws and release clamping force slowly.

tools.name: MIG Torch
tools.purpose: Places short tack welds at the overlapped frame-side corners.
tools.usageNotes: Use brief tacks in the authored order so the panel stays square and heat input remains controlled.
tools.safetyNotes: Use welding PPE, protect nearby surfaces, and maintain ventilation before striking an arc.

tools.name: Angle Grinder
tools.purpose: Cleans proud weld metal and spatter from the frame faces that will later carry rail and bracket hardware.
tools.usageNotes: Use light cleanup passes on the authored upper joints so later mounting faces stay usable without removing structural material.
tools.safetyNotes: Use eye and face protection, keep the guard in place, and control sparks away from the work area.

tools.name: 5 mm Metric Allen Key
tools.purpose: Starts and tightens the X-axis fitting fasteners.

tools.name: Pliers
tools.purpose: Pulls the X-axis belt to final tension.

tools.name: 2.5 mm Metric Hex Key
tools.purpose: Secures the small holder-side fasteners during the first Titan Aero module build.

tools.name: Small Screwdriver
tools.purpose: Secures the blower attachment during the first Titan Aero module build.

tools.name: Power Drill
tools.purpose: Drills pilot holes and drives screws during D3D frame and axis assembly.
tools.usageNotes: Use a 3 mm bit for pilot holes in flat stock. Switch to a driver bit for M3/M5 screws.
tools.safetyNotes: Wear eye protection. Secure the workpiece before drilling.
tools.defaultOrientationHint: Hold grip with trigger facing down, bit pointing forward.

tools.name: Box Cutter
tools.purpose: Cuts cable ties, opens packaging, and trims wire insulation during wiring steps.
tools.usageNotes: Extend the blade one notch only. Cut away from fingers.
tools.safetyNotes: Retract blade immediately after use. Keep fingers behind the blade guard.
tools.defaultOrientationHint: Hold handle with blade pointing forward along +Z.

tools.name: Sandpaper (220 Grit)
tools.purpose: Smooths rough weld spatter and 3D-printed surfaces before assembly.
tools.usageNotes: Sand in one direction along the grain. Replace when the grit loads with metal dust.
tools.safetyNotes: Wear a dust mask when sanding metal or plastic. Keep away from weld sparks.
tools.defaultOrientationHint: Hold flat with abrasive side facing the work surface.

tools.name: Lubricant Spray
tools.purpose: Lubricates smooth rod surfaces and linear bearing housings to reduce friction.
tools.usageNotes: Apply a thin coat to the rod surface. Wipe off excess before inserting bearings.
tools.safetyNotes: Use in a ventilated area. Avoid spraying near open flames or electronics.
tools.defaultOrientationHint: Hold upright with nozzle pointing toward the work area.

tools.name: Drill Bits (3 mm + 6 mm)
tools.purpose: 3 mm bit for pilot holes in flat stock; 6 mm bit for larger clearance holes.
tools.usageNotes: Insert bit fully into the chuck and tighten before drilling. Use cutting fluid on metal.
tools.safetyNotes: Discard bent or dull bits. Never touch a spinning bit.
tools.defaultOrientationHint: Bits pointing forward along +Z, shank end at the back.

steps.name: Place all four bottom frame side bars
steps.instructionText: Place all four bars of the bottom frame side into their target positions. Keep each bar flat and preserve the 1 inch overlap that produces the 14 inch outer square.
steps.whyItMattersText: All four edges of the bottom frame side must stay true to the 14 inch square geometry or the final D3D frame will accumulate twist before assembly.

guidance.instructionText: Place all four bars of the bottom frame side into their target positions. Keep each bar flat and preserve the 1 inch overlap that produces the 14 inch outer square.
guidance.whyItMattersText: All four edges of the bottom frame side must stay true to the 14 inch square geometry or the final D3D frame will accumulate twist before assembly.

steps.name: Verify the bottom frame side is square
steps.instructionText: Equip the framing square and check the upper-left corner marker on the bottom frame side before any clamp load or heat is introduced.
steps.whyItMattersText: Verifying the bottom frame side before welding catches layout error while the bars can still be shifted freely.

requiredToolActions.successMessage: Bottom Frame Side checked for square.
requiredToolActions.failureMessage: Equip the framing square and use it on the pulsing corner marker.

guidance.instructionText: Equip the framing square and check the upper-left corner marker on the bottom frame side before any clamp load or heat is introduced.
guidance.whyItMattersText: Verifying the bottom frame side before welding catches layout error while the bars can still be shifted freely.

steps.name: Clamp the bottom frame side
steps.instructionText: Equip the locking clamp and pin the verified bottom frame side at the center hold-down marker so the bars stay flat while the corner tacks go in.
steps.whyItMattersText: Stable hold-down prevents the overlapped bars from walking out of square once tack weld heat is added.

requiredToolActions.successMessage: Bottom Frame Side pinned flat for tack welding.
requiredToolActions.failureMessage: Equip the locking clamp and use it on the pulsing hold-down marker.

guidance.instructionText: Equip the locking clamp and pin the verified bottom frame side at the center hold-down marker so the bars stay flat while the corner tacks go in.
guidance.whyItMattersText: Stable hold-down prevents the overlapped bars from walking out of square once tack weld heat is added.

steps.name: Tack-weld the bottom frame side corners
steps.instructionText: Equip the MIG torch and place four short tack welds on the bottom frame side in this order: upper-left, lower-right, upper-right, then lower-left. Keep the panel flat and avoid a long heat soak.
steps.whyItMattersText: Short tacks lock the overlap geometry in place while limiting distortion before the full frame cube is assembled.

requiredToolActions.successMessage: Bottom Frame Side upper-left corner tack complete.
requiredToolActions.failureMessage: Equip the MIG torch and use it on the highlighted corner tack target.

requiredToolActions.successMessage: Bottom Frame Side lower-right corner tack complete.
requiredToolActions.failureMessage: Equip the MIG torch and use it on the highlighted corner tack target.

requiredToolActions.successMessage: Bottom Frame Side upper-right corner tack complete.
requiredToolActions.failureMessage: Equip the MIG torch and use it on the highlighted corner tack target.

requiredToolActions.successMessage: Bottom Frame Side lower-left corner tack complete.
requiredToolActions.failureMessage: Equip the MIG torch and use it on the highlighted corner tack target.

guidance.instructionText: Equip the MIG torch and place four short tack welds on the bottom frame side in this order: upper-left, lower-right, upper-right, then lower-left. Keep the panel flat and avoid a long heat soak.
guidance.whyItMattersText: Short tacks lock the overlap geometry in place while limiting distortion before the full frame cube is assembled.

reinforcement.milestoneMessage: First frame side complete! You've learned the full Place-Square-Clamp-Weld cycle.
reinforcement.safetyNote: Keep the MIG torch tip clean between tacks. A dirty tip causes spatter and weak penetration.
reinforcement.counterfactualText: Skipping the diagonal order would concentrate heat on one side, warping the panel before the last corner locks.

steps.name: Place all four top frame side bars
steps.instructionText: Place the four top frame bars. Same 1-inch overlap, same 14-inch square.
steps.whyItMattersText: Same geometry rules apply â€” keep the 14-inch outer square consistent across every side.

guidance.instructionText: Place the four top frame bars. Same 1-inch overlap, same 14-inch square.
guidance.whyItMattersText: Same geometry rules apply â€” keep the 14-inch outer square consistent across every side.

steps.name: Verify the top frame side is square
steps.instructionText: Check the corner with the framing square before clamping.
steps.whyItMattersText: Catching errors before clamping saves time.

requiredToolActions.successMessage: Top Frame Side checked for square.
requiredToolActions.failureMessage: Equip the framing square and use it on the pulsing corner marker.

guidance.instructionText: Check the corner with the framing square before clamping.
guidance.whyItMattersText: Catching errors before clamping saves time.

steps.name: Clamp the top frame side
steps.instructionText: Pin the panel flat at the center hold-down.
steps.whyItMattersText: Hold-down keeps the bars from shifting during welding.

requiredToolActions.successMessage: Top Frame Side pinned flat for tack welding.
requiredToolActions.failureMessage: Equip the locking clamp and use it on the pulsing hold-down marker.

guidance.instructionText: Pin the panel flat at the center hold-down.

steps.name: Tack-weld the top frame side corners
steps.instructionText: Tack the four corners: upper-left, lower-right, upper-right, lower-left.
steps.whyItMattersText: Diagonal order balances heat and prevents warping.

steps.name: Place the bottom frame side
steps.instructionText: Move the completed bottom frame side from its fabrication station to the cube bottom target.
steps.whyItMattersText: The bottom panel establishes the base footprint for the remaining frame sides.

steps.name: Place the left frame side
steps.instructionText: Lift the completed left frame side and place it on the left cube target.
steps.whyItMattersText: The left panel establishes one of the first vertical faces of the printer frame.

steps.name: Tack-weld left panel to bottom
steps.instructionText: Equip the MIG torch and place a short tack weld at the front-left and rear-left cube corners to secure the left panel to the bottom.
steps.whyItMattersText: Tacking each panel immediately after placement prevents it from shifting or falling as the next panels are added.

requiredToolActions.failureMessage: Equip the MIG torch and use it on the highlighted target.
Equip the MIG torch and use it on the highlighted target.

steps.name: Place the right frame side
steps.instructionText: Lift the completed right frame side and place it on the right cube target.
steps.whyItMattersText: The right panel locks the cube width opposite the left face.

steps.name: Tack-weld right panel to bottom
steps.instructionText: Place a short tack weld at the front-right and rear-right cube corners to secure the right panel to the bottom.
steps.whyItMattersText: Both vertical side panels are now tacked to the bottom, forming a rigid U-channel that captures the front and rear panels.

requiredToolActions.failureMessage: Equip the MIG torch and use it on the highlighted target.
Equip the MIG torch and use it on the highlighted target.

steps.name: Place the front frame side
steps.instructionText: Lift the completed front frame side and place it on the front cube target.
steps.whyItMattersText: The front panel starts closing the cube depth and establishes the front opening plane.

steps.name: Tack-weld front panel to side panels
steps.instructionText: Place a short tack weld at the front-left and front-right mid-height edges to secure the front panel to the left and right side panels.
steps.whyItMattersText: Tacking immediately after placement prevents the front panel from shifting when the rear panel is added.

requiredToolActions.failureMessage: Equip the MIG torch and use it on the highlighted target.
Equip the MIG torch and use it on the highlighted target.

steps.name: Place the rear frame side
steps.instructionText: Lift the completed rear frame side and place it on the rear cube target.
steps.whyItMattersText: The rear panel completes the four vertical faces before the top closes the frame.

steps.name: Tack-weld rear panel to side panels
steps.instructionText: Place a short tack weld at the rear-left and rear-right mid-height edges to secure the rear panel to the left and right side panels.
steps.whyItMattersText: All four vertical panels are now tacked at their mid-height edges. The cube base is rigid enough for the top panel.

requiredToolActions.failureMessage: Equip the MIG torch and use it on the highlighted target.
Equip the MIG torch and use it on the highlighted target.

steps.name: Place the top frame side
steps.instructionText: Move the completed top frame side to the upper cube target to close the frame layout.
steps.whyItMattersText: The top panel completes the first-pass cube stack and exposes any remaining alignment issues before final joining.

steps.name: Tack-weld top panel to vertical panels
steps.instructionText: Place short tacks at all four upper cube corners in diagonal order: front-left, rear-right, front-right, rear-left. This locks the top panel to all four vertical sides.
steps.whyItMattersText: All eight corners are now tacked. The cube is structurally held and ready for squareness verification before seam welding.

requiredToolActions.failureMessage: Equip the MIG torch and use it on the highlighted target.
Equip the MIG torch and use it on the highlighted target.
Equip the MIG torch and use it on the highlighted target.
Equip the MIG torch and use it on the highlighted target.

steps.name: Verify the tacked cube is square
steps.instructionText: Equip the framing square and check the top-front-left cube corner. All six panels are now tacked in place â€” verify the cube is square before running the seam welds.
steps.whyItMattersText: This catches accumulated twist or panel drift before the seam welds permanently lock the cube geometry.

requiredToolActions.failureMessage: Equip the framing square and use it on the highlighted cube corner marker.

steps.name: Seam-weld the 4 lower cube edges
steps.instructionText: Run a continuous weld bead along each of the four lower vertical corner edges. Start from the bottom and travel upward on each seam.
steps.whyItMattersText: Seam welds create the permanent structural bond between frame sides. A continuous bead distributes load evenly along the full joint length.

requiredToolActions.failureMessage: Equip the MIG torch and use it on the highlighted target.
Equip the MIG torch and use it on the highlighted target.
Equip the MIG torch and use it on the highlighted target.
Equip the MIG torch and use it on the highlighted target.

steps.name: Seam-weld the 4 upper cube edges
steps.instructionText: Run a continuous weld bead along each of the four upper vertical corner edges. Same bottom-to-top travel direction.
steps.whyItMattersText: Upper seams complete the cube weld structure. All eight vertical edges are now fully welded.

requiredToolActions.failureMessage: Equip the MIG torch and use it on the highlighted target.
Equip the MIG torch and use it on the highlighted target.
Equip the MIG torch and use it on the highlighted target.
Equip the MIG torch and use it on the highlighted target.

reinforcement.milestoneMessage: All eight cube seams welded. The frame is structurally joined.
reinforcement.safetyNote: Let the welds cool before grinding. Hot steel grinds unevenly and throws hotter sparks.

steps.name: Grind the 4 upper cube seam joints
steps.instructionText: Equip the angle grinder and clean up the four upper corner seam joints. Remove spatter and proud weld bead so the upper brackets will sit flush.
steps.whyItMattersText: Upper bracket faces must be flat. Weld spatter or proud bead under a bracket creates a gap that lets the bolt loosen over time.

requiredToolActions.failureMessage: Equip the angle grinder and use it on the highlighted joint.
Equip the angle grinder and use it on the highlighted joint.
Equip the angle grinder and use it on the highlighted joint.
Equip the angle grinder and use it on the highlighted joint.

steps.name: Verify cube is square after welding
steps.instructionText: Use the framing square on the front-left and rear-right cube corners to confirm weld heat did not rack the frame.
steps.whyItMattersText: Weld shrinkage can pull corners out of square. Catching it now is far easier to correct than after brackets are installed.

requiredToolActions.failureMessage: Equip the framing square and use it on the highlighted corner.
Equip the framing square and use it on the highlighted corner.

reinforcement.milestoneMessage: Frame cube welded, ground, and verified square. Ready for corner brackets.

steps.name: Place 4 lower corner brackets
steps.instructionText: Slide a 3D-printed corner bracket onto each of the four lower cube corners â€” the three bracket arms wrap around the outside faces of the three frame tubes meeting at that corner. Push each bracket flush until it seats against the frame. Thread two M6x18 bolts per bracket in by hand until finger-tight â€” do not use the allen key yet. Work in this order: front-left, rear-right, front-right, rear-left.
steps.whyItMattersText: Starting with the lower corners establishes the base geometry of the cube before the upper corners are locked in.

steps.name: Snug lower bracket bolts with allen key
steps.instructionText: Use the M5 allen key to snug all 8 bolts across the four lower brackets. Snug means the bolt is fully seated and the bracket cannot rock on the frame â€” not final torque. Keep the frame slightly adjustable for the upper brackets to align.
steps.whyItMattersText: Leaving bolts finger-tight allows minor adjustments when the upper brackets are added, ensuring the cube stays square.

requiredToolActions.failureMessage: Use the allen key on the highlighted lower bracket bolts.
Use the allen key on the highlighted lower bracket bolts.
Use the allen key on the highlighted lower bracket bolts.
Use the allen key on the highlighted lower bracket bolts.

steps.name: Place 4 upper corner brackets
steps.instructionText: Slide a corner bracket onto each of the four upper cube corners. Push flush until seated, then thread two M6x18 bolts per bracket in by hand. Work in the same order: front-left, rear-right, front-right, rear-left.
steps.whyItMattersText: Upper brackets complete the structural lock of all eight cube corners.

requiredToolActions.failureMessage: Equip the allen key and place the bracket at the highlighted upper corner.
Equip the allen key and place the bracket at the highlighted upper corner.
Equip the allen key and place the bracket at the highlighted upper corner.
Equip the allen key and place the bracket at the highlighted upper corner.

steps.name: Snug upper bracket bolts with allen key
steps.instructionText: Use the allen key to snug all 8 bolts on the four upper brackets. All 8 brackets should now hold the cube shape loosely â€” bolts seated but frame still adjustable for squaring.
steps.whyItMattersText: With all brackets finger-tight the cube can still be nudged square before the final tightening pass.

requiredToolActions.failureMessage: Use the allen key on the highlighted upper bracket bolts.
Use the allen key on the highlighted upper bracket bolts.
Use the allen key on the highlighted upper bracket bolts.
Use the allen key on the highlighted upper bracket bolts.

steps.name: Verify bracketed cube is square
steps.instructionText: Equip the framing square and check the top-front-left cube corner. Tap any out-of-square sides into alignment while the bolts are still finger-tight.
steps.whyItMattersText: This is the last chance to adjust the cube geometry before the bolts are fully tightened.

requiredToolActions.failureMessage: Equip the framing square and check the highlighted cube corner.

steps.name: Final-tighten all bracket bolts
steps.instructionText: Use the allen key to fully tighten all 16 bracket bolts across all 8 corners. Work in alternating diagonal pairs (lower front-left then upper rear-right, etc.) to distribute clamping force evenly.
steps.whyItMattersText: Even tightening prevents one corner from pulling the frame out of square while the opposite corner is still loose.

requiredToolActions.failureMessage: Use the allen key to fully tighten the highlighted bracket bolts.
Use the allen key to fully tighten the highlighted bracket bolts.
Use the allen key to fully tighten the highlighted bracket bolts.
Use the allen key to fully tighten the highlighted bracket bolts.
Use the allen key to fully tighten the highlighted bracket bolts.
Use the allen key to fully tighten the highlighted bracket bolts.
Use the allen key to fully tighten the highlighted bracket bolts.
Use the allen key to fully tighten the highlighted bracket bolts.

steps.name: Verify cube after final tightening
steps.instructionText: Equip the framing square and recheck the top-front-left cube corner after all bolts are fully tightened. Confirm the frame stayed true through the tightening sequence.
steps.whyItMattersText: A post-tightening square check catches any shift introduced by bolt clamping forces.

requiredToolActions.failureMessage: Equip the framing square and check the highlighted cube corner.

steps.name: Recheck opposite cube corners
steps.instructionText: Equip the framing square and verify both the top-front-left and top-rear-right cube corners. This cross-check confirms the cube is true on opposite diagonals.
steps.whyItMattersText: Checking opposite corners catches twist that a single-corner check might miss.

requiredToolActions.failureMessage: Equip the framing square and check the highlighted corner.
Equip the framing square and check the highlighted corner.

steps.name: Accept the frame for motion hardware
steps.instructionText: Make a final acceptance check on the bracketed frame and continue only when it is ready for the first rail, gantry, and motion-hardware installation steps.
steps.whyItMattersText: Closing the frame phase explicitly helps prevent structural errors from propagating into the next subsystem.

steps.name: Mount the Y-left axis to the frame
steps.instructionText: Place the completed Y-left axis unit onto the welded frame at the authored left-side mount.

steps.name: Confirm the Y-left axis seats cleanly on the frame
steps.instructionText: Inspect the mounted Y-left axis and confirm its travel direction is clear and the unit sits flat on the frame-side mount.

steps.name: Mount the Y-right axis to the frame
steps.instructionText: Place the completed Y-right axis unit onto the welded frame at the authored right-side mount.

steps.name: Confirm the Y-axis pair establishes the X-axis span
steps.instructionText: Inspect the mounted Y-left and Y-right axes together and confirm they establish a clean span for the X-axis between them.

steps.name: Tighten the X-axis idler screws
steps.instructionText: Tighten the X-axis idler screws while the module is still staged in the work area.

steps.name: Place the X-axis assembly with the idler side anchored on Y-right
steps.instructionText: Place the X-axis idler side, rod pair, motor-holder side, and the two Y-right M6x30 bolts into their pre-fit positions so the idler side anchors on Y-right first.

steps.name: Snug the Y-right M6x30 anchor bolts
steps.instructionText: Use the metric Allen key to snug the two Y-right M6x30 bolts so the idler side stays anchored while the motor-holder side still remains adjustable.

steps.name: Loosely start the X-axis motor-holder bolts on the Y-left carriage
steps.instructionText: Place the two Y-left M6x30 motor-holder bolts by hand only loosely so the X-axis span can still be adjusted.

steps.name: Lengthen the X-axis to fit tightly between the Y axes
steps.instructionText: Drag the yellow motor-holder side left along the fit guide until it reaches the green final-fit marker and seats against the Y-left side.

steps.name: Lock the Y-left mount bolts and motor-holder screws
steps.instructionText: After the X-axis span is seated tightly, tighten the two Y-left M6x30 mount bolts first, then lock the motor-holder-side internal screws.

steps.name: Tension the X-axis belt
steps.instructionText: Pull the X-axis belt to final hand tension only after the span and fasteners are locked.

steps.name: Reinsert the belt peg
steps.instructionText: Place the belt peg back into the retainer once the belt is tensioned.

steps.name: Check X-axis tightness and travel
steps.instructionText: Inspect the fitted X-axis and confirm it can travel along the mounted Y axes without binding, without a visible gap at the left interface, and with the Y-left end-stop relationship preserved.

steps.name: Stage the Titan Aero core
steps.instructionText: Place the Titan Aero core into the extruder work zone so the nozzle, fan, and sensor-side parts can be added in sequence.

steps.name: Attach the nozzle assembly
steps.instructionText: Place the nozzle assembly onto the staged Titan Aero core before any cooling or sensor-side hardware is added.

steps.name: Place the print-cooling blower
steps.instructionText: Place the 5015 blower on the fan side of the nozzle module before securing its mount points.

steps.name: Secure the blower attachment
steps.instructionText: Tighten the blower-side fasteners only after the blower body is seated on the nozzle module.

steps.name: Place the sensor holder
steps.instructionText: Place the sensor holder on the nozzle module after the blower side is established.

steps.name: Secure the sensor holder
steps.instructionText: Tighten the small holder-side fasteners so the sensor holder is fixed before the sensor is inserted.

steps.name: Place the 8 mm sensor
steps.instructionText: Place the 8 mm sensor into the holder after the holder is secured.

steps.name: Check nozzle-module clearance
steps.instructionText: Inspect the completed nozzle module and confirm the nozzle, blower side, and sensor side all keep clear service space for later carriage integration.

steps.name: Stage the simplified carriage
steps.instructionText: Place the simplified carriage into the carriage-mount work zone before the printed bracket and top plate are added.

steps.name: Place the Titan Aero mount bracket
steps.instructionText: Place the stock Titan Aero mount bracket onto the staged simplified carriage before any fasteners are tightened.

steps.name: Secure the mount bracket
steps.instructionText: Use the metric Allen key to tighten the two bracket fasteners only after the bracket sits flat on the simplified carriage.

steps.name: Place the Titan Aero mount top plate
steps.instructionText: Place the top plate over the bracket stack so the carriage-side mount can be locked before the completed nozzle module is added.

steps.name: Secure the mount top plate
steps.instructionText: Use the small hex driver to tighten the top-plate fasteners so the bracket stack is locked before the completed nozzle module is mounted.

steps.name: Mount the completed nozzle module to the carriage stack
steps.instructionText: Move the completed Titan Aero nozzle module onto the prepared carriage-side bracket stack. Keep the module square to the mount and let the rigid subassembly target guide the final seated pose.

steps.name: Check carriage-mount clearance and access
steps.instructionText: Inspect the completed carriage-side Titan Aero mount and confirm the body, nozzle, and sensor side all keep basic service access before later printer-wide integration is authored.

steps.name: Stage the X-axis carriage side
steps.instructionText: Place the X-axis carriage-side interface onto the rod-pair work area so the completed extruder carriage unit has a real printer-side receiving body.

steps.name: Mount the completed extruder carriage unit onto the X-axis interface
steps.instructionText: Move the completed extruder carriage unit onto the X-axis carriage-side interface and let the rigid mount target guide the first seated printer-side position.

steps.name: Close the half carriage over the mounted extruder unit
steps.instructionText: Bring the half carriage in square to the mounted carriage side so the clamp-side body closes over the rod path and captures the mounted extruder unit.

steps.name: Check extruder X-axis travel clearance
steps.instructionText: Inspect the mounted extruder carriage and confirm it can move along the X-axis without visible interference at the left and right travel limits and with nozzle-side clearance preserved.

steps.name: Stage the heatbed body
steps.instructionText: Place the heatbed body into the printer so the first mechanical bed envelope is established before the retainers are added.

steps.name: Place the left snapbuckle
steps.instructionText: Place the left snapbuckle retainer onto the left bed-side contact point after the heatbed body is seated.

steps.name: Place the right snapbuckle
steps.instructionText: Place the right snapbuckle retainer onto the right bed-side contact point so the first mechanical bed path is held symmetrically.

steps.name: Check heated-bed envelope clearance
steps.instructionText: Inspect the staged heatbed body and retainer pair and confirm the bed envelope stays centered under the nozzle path with clear left and right margins.

steps.name: Assembly complete
steps.instructionText: All parts are now in their final assembled positions. Rotate the view to inspect the completed D3D printer stage 1 assembly.

steps.name: Lay out axis pieces
steps.instructionText: Gather and lay out all axis pieces: two carriage half-pieces, two idler half-pieces, two motor holder half-pieces, the stepper motor, both guide rods, and all hardware (M3x25 screws, M6x18 and M6x30 bolts, nuts).

steps.name: Clean excess plastic from holes
steps.instructionText: Use a box cutter or cordless power drill to clear excess plastic from all semi-circle rod holes in the carriage, idler, and motor holder half-pieces. Holes must be smooth so rods insert freely and belts do not rub.

steps.name: QC: verify all plastic pieces are clean
steps.instructionText: Verify: each carriage half has 4 clean semi-circles, each motor holder half has 6, each idler half has 8. All M6 bolt holes and M3 screw holes are clear of extra plastic.

steps.name: Place 4 bearings in carriage half
steps.instructionText: Place four linear bearings into one carriage half-piece with the flanges facing outward (away from each other).

steps.name: Shake test: bearings must not rattle
steps.instructionText: Add the second carriage half on top and compress the two halves firmly. Shake the closed carriage — bearings must not rattle. If they do, the fit is too loose; adjust or swap bearings until snug.

steps.name: Rod slide test: slight resistance only
steps.instructionText: Insert a guide rod through the carriage and hold it vertical. The rod should drop through with slight resistance, not freely. Too free: wrap each bearing in one layer of electrical tape and retest.

steps.name: Bolt carriage halves together
steps.instructionText: Secure both halves with M6x18 bolts on top and M6x30 bolts on bottom. Critical alignment: the small ribbed belt hole must sit directly beside the large smooth belt hole. Wrong alignment mismatches the peg holes.

steps.name: Insert M6x18 bolt into idler half
steps.instructionText: Begin assembling the idler: place an M6x18 bolt through one idler half-piece from the outside.

steps.name: Stack two flanged bearings onto bolt
steps.instructionText: Insert two bearing pieces one on top of the other onto the bolt, flanges facing outward (away from each other).

steps.name: Align idler rod holes before closing
steps.instructionText: Hold the second idler half against the first and confirm the circular rod holes on both halves line up correctly before pressing them together.

steps.name: Bolt idler halves and secure bearing
steps.instructionText: Secure the idler with an M6x18 bolt through the top and one M6x30 bolt in the belt-side hole. Tighten until firm.

steps.name: Mount pulley on motor shaft with spacer
steps.instructionText: Rest the thin spacer on the motor housing, then seat the pulley on the shaft above it. Tighten the first set screw with an Allen key — the spacer keeps the pulley off the housing.

steps.name: Lock pulley set screw until it pops
steps.instructionText: Remove the spacer. Tighten the second set screw as firmly as possible, pressing near the Allen key midpoint. A soft "pop" means fully seated. Whether or not you hear it, tighten as hard as you can.

steps.name: Load motor holder half with nuts and M6x30 bolt
steps.instructionText: Orient the motor holder half with the rectangular belt channel facing you. Insert one M6x30 bolt and three nuts into the holes now — the motor will cover them and block access later.

steps.name: Insert belt in motor holder channel
steps.instructionText: Insert the belt into the channel in the motor holder half-piece with the toothed side facing inward toward where the pulley will sit.

steps.name: Close motor holder halves together
steps.instructionText: Press the two motor holder halves together with the belt seated in the channel.

steps.name: Confirm belt runs smoothly on pulley
steps.instructionText: Hold the motor in position against the holder and give the belt a gentle pull — it should slide along the motor pulley. If it does not, reseat the belt in the channel.

steps.name: Attach motor with 4 M3x25 screws
steps.instructionText: Insert the first M3x25 motor screw and tighten 3/4 of the way. Insert the remaining three screws and tighten each the same way — leave slightly loose so the motor retains flex for belt tensioning later.

steps.name: Insert M6x18 bolts into motor holder
steps.instructionText: Insert M6x18 bolts into the three remaining motor holder holes and tighten each into the nuts with an electric drill.

steps.name: Dangle test: motor holder hangs securely
steps.instructionText: Hold the complete motor assembly by one of its bolts. It should hang without wobble or separation. This confirms bolts are fully seated before rod insertion.

steps.name: Insert rods into idler, ends flush
steps.instructionText: Insert both guide rods into the completed idler piece. Rod ends must be flush against the bottom of the idler. If rods are hard to insert, loosen the idler bolts, slide rods in, then re-tighten.

steps.name: Tighten short idler bolts onto rods
steps.instructionText: Tighten the two shorter idler bolts with an electric drill to grip the rods firmly. Leave the longer bolt loose — it will be used to attach the axis to the frame.

steps.name: Slide carriage onto rods
steps.instructionText: Slide the completed carriage onto the rods with the long bolt ends closer to the idler. This orientation maximizes the travel distance.

steps.name: Push motor holder onto rods
steps.instructionText: Push the motor holder piece onto the rods so the motor faces the carriage bolt heads. Loosen bolts if needed to slide on, then re-tighten.

steps.name: QC: rods flush, carriage slides freely
steps.instructionText: Check that rod ends are still flush at the idler bottom. Slide the carriage the full rod length — it should move with only slight bearing resistance.

steps.name: Thread belt through large carriage hole
steps.instructionText: Inspect the carriage: one belt hole is large and smooth, the other small and ribbed. Insert one belt end through the large smooth hole with the toothed side facing inward.

steps.name: Route belt around idler bearing
steps.instructionText: Feed the belt around the bearing inside the idler. Tip: curve the belt slightly in the direction of travel first — it navigates around the bearing more easily.

steps.name: Thread belt back through small carriage hole
steps.instructionText: Once the belt is around the idler bearing, pull it back through the smaller ribbed carriage hole.

steps.name: Confirm belt peg orientation before locking
steps.instructionText: Thread the belt end through a belt peg. Confirm: the foot of the peg faces away from the toothed side of the belt and away from the axis center. Wrong orientation prevents the peg from locking correctly.

steps.name: Lock first belt peg
steps.instructionText: Insert the belt end approximately 3/4 inch into the small ribbed belt hole, then simultaneously insert the peg into the same hole and press firmly until it locks.

steps.name: Insert second peg loosely
steps.instructionText: Insert the second peg directly across from the first. Hand-tighten the belt by pulling it through the first peg as much as you can easily. Leave the second peg loose — it will be fully tensioned after mounting to the frame.

steps.name: Travel test: carriage moves smoothly with belt
steps.instructionText: Slide the carriage along the full rod length with the belt installed. It should move smoothly without binding. If it binds, check the belt is not twisted and both carriage holes are correctly threaded.

steps.name: Label completed Y-Left Axis and set aside
steps.instructionText: Use masking tape and a marker to label this assembly as "Y-Left Axis". Set it aside in a safe place. It is complete and ready for frame mounting.

steps.name: Gather endstop switch and holder piece
steps.instructionText: Gather the endstop microswitch and its 3D-printed holder piece. This endstop step applies to this axis only.

steps.name: Slide endstop board into holder crevice
steps.instructionText: Slide the endstop board into the crevice of the holder piece. The switch arm must face toward the arrowhead direction of the holder, away from the mounting legs. The bottom of the board should be flush with the bottom of the holder.

steps.name: Super glue endstop into holder
steps.instructionText: Dab super glue along the side edges of the crevice. Do not apply glue to the metal switch arm or any board contacts.

steps.name: Prep motor holder holes for endstop
steps.instructionText: Dab super glue into the two motor holder holes facing the belt (top-left and bottom-left when the motor faces you).

steps.name: Press endstop holder onto motor holder
steps.instructionText: Press and hold the endstop holder piece firmly against the motor holder piece for at least 30 seconds until bonded.

steps.name: Reinforce endstop joint with super glue
steps.instructionText: Add super glue along all joint edges between the endstop holder and the motor holder to strengthen the connection.

steps.name: Lay out axis pieces
steps.instructionText: Gather and lay out all axis pieces: two carriage half-pieces, two idler half-pieces, two motor holder half-pieces, the stepper motor, both guide rods, and all hardware (M3x25 screws, M6x18 and M6x30 bolts, nuts).

steps.name: Clean excess plastic from holes
steps.instructionText: Use a box cutter or cordless power drill to clear excess plastic from all semi-circle rod holes in the carriage, idler, and motor holder half-pieces. Holes must be smooth so rods insert freely and belts do not rub.

steps.name: QC: verify all plastic pieces are clean
steps.instructionText: Verify: each carriage half has 4 clean semi-circles, each motor holder half has 6, each idler half has 8. All M6 bolt holes and M3 screw holes are clear of extra plastic.

steps.name: Place 4 bearings in carriage half
steps.instructionText: Place four linear bearings into one carriage half-piece with the flanges facing outward (away from each other).

steps.name: Shake test: bearings must not rattle
steps.instructionText: Add the second carriage half on top and compress the two halves firmly. Shake the closed carriage — bearings must not rattle. If they do, the fit is too loose; adjust or swap bearings until snug.

steps.name: Rod slide test: slight resistance only
steps.instructionText: Insert a guide rod through the carriage and hold it vertical. The rod should drop through with slight resistance, not freely. Too free: wrap each bearing in one layer of electrical tape and retest.

steps.name: Bolt carriage halves together
steps.instructionText: Secure both halves with M6x18 bolts on top and M6x30 bolts on bottom. Critical alignment: the small ribbed belt hole must sit directly beside the large smooth belt hole. Wrong alignment mismatches the peg holes.

steps.name: Insert M6x18 bolt into idler half
steps.instructionText: Begin assembling the idler: place an M6x18 bolt through one idler half-piece from the outside.

steps.name: Stack two flanged bearings onto bolt
steps.instructionText: Insert two bearing pieces one on top of the other onto the bolt, flanges facing outward (away from each other).

steps.name: Align idler rod holes before closing
steps.instructionText: Hold the second idler half against the first and confirm the circular rod holes on both halves line up correctly before pressing them together.

steps.name: Bolt idler halves and secure bearing
steps.instructionText: Secure the idler with an M6x18 bolt through the top and one M6x30 bolt in the belt-side hole. Tighten until firm.

steps.name: Mount pulley on motor shaft with spacer
steps.instructionText: Rest the thin spacer on the motor housing, then seat the pulley on the shaft above it. Tighten the first set screw with an Allen key — the spacer keeps the pulley off the housing.

steps.name: Lock pulley set screw until it pops
steps.instructionText: Remove the spacer. Tighten the second set screw as firmly as possible, pressing near the Allen key midpoint. A soft "pop" means fully seated. Whether or not you hear it, tighten as hard as you can.

steps.name: Load motor holder half with nuts and M6x30 bolt
steps.instructionText: Orient the motor holder half with the rectangular belt channel facing you. Insert one M6x30 bolt and three nuts into the holes now — the motor will cover them and block access later.

steps.name: Insert belt in motor holder channel
steps.instructionText: Insert the belt into the channel in the motor holder half-piece with the toothed side facing inward toward where the pulley will sit.

steps.name: Close motor holder halves together
steps.instructionText: Press the two motor holder halves together with the belt seated in the channel.

steps.name: Confirm belt runs smoothly on pulley
steps.instructionText: Hold the motor in position against the holder and give the belt a gentle pull — it should slide along the motor pulley. If it does not, reseat the belt in the channel.

steps.name: Attach motor with 4 M3x25 screws
steps.instructionText: Insert the first M3x25 motor screw and tighten 3/4 of the way. Insert the remaining three screws and tighten each the same way — leave slightly loose so the motor retains flex for belt tensioning later.

steps.name: Insert M6x18 bolts into motor holder
steps.instructionText: Insert M6x18 bolts into the three remaining motor holder holes and tighten each into the nuts with an electric drill.

steps.name: Dangle test: motor holder hangs securely
steps.instructionText: Hold the complete motor assembly by one of its bolts. It should hang without wobble or separation. This confirms bolts are fully seated before rod insertion.

steps.name: Insert rods into idler, ends flush
steps.instructionText: Insert both guide rods into the completed idler piece. Rod ends must be flush against the bottom of the idler. If rods are hard to insert, loosen the idler bolts, slide rods in, then re-tighten.

steps.name: Tighten short idler bolts onto rods
steps.instructionText: Tighten the two shorter idler bolts with an electric drill to grip the rods firmly. Leave the longer bolt loose — it will be used to attach the axis to the frame.

steps.name: Slide carriage onto rods
steps.instructionText: Slide the completed carriage onto the rods with the long bolt ends closer to the idler. This orientation maximizes the travel distance.

steps.name: Push motor holder onto rods
steps.instructionText: Push the motor holder piece onto the rods so the motor faces the carriage bolt heads. Loosen bolts if needed to slide on, then re-tighten.

steps.name: QC: rods flush, carriage slides freely
steps.instructionText: Check that rod ends are still flush at the idler bottom. Slide the carriage the full rod length — it should move with only slight bearing resistance.

steps.name: Thread belt through large carriage hole
steps.instructionText: Inspect the carriage: one belt hole is large and smooth, the other small and ribbed. Insert one belt end through the large smooth hole with the toothed side facing inward.

steps.name: Route belt around idler bearing
steps.instructionText: Feed the belt around the bearing inside the idler. Tip: curve the belt slightly in the direction of travel first — it navigates around the bearing more easily.

steps.name: Thread belt back through small carriage hole
steps.instructionText: Once the belt is around the idler bearing, pull it back through the smaller ribbed carriage hole.

steps.name: Confirm belt peg orientation before locking
steps.instructionText: Thread the belt end through a belt peg. Confirm: the foot of the peg faces away from the toothed side of the belt and away from the axis center. Wrong orientation prevents the peg from locking correctly.

steps.name: Lock first belt peg
steps.instructionText: Insert the belt end approximately 3/4 inch into the small ribbed belt hole, then simultaneously insert the peg into the same hole and press firmly until it locks.

steps.name: Insert second peg loosely
steps.instructionText: Insert the second peg directly across from the first. Hand-tighten the belt by pulling it through the first peg as much as you can easily. Leave the second peg loose — it will be fully tensioned after mounting to the frame.

steps.name: Travel test: carriage moves smoothly with belt
steps.instructionText: Slide the carriage along the full rod length with the belt installed. It should move smoothly without binding. If it binds, check the belt is not twisted and both carriage holes are correctly threaded.
