steps.instructionText: Slide the carriage along the full rod length with the belt installed. It should move smoothly without binding. If it binds, check the belt is not twisted and both carriage holes are correctly threaded.

steps.name: Label completed Z-Back Axis and set aside
steps.instructionText: Use masking tape and a marker to label this assembly as "Z-Back Axis". Set it aside in a safe place. It is complete and ready for frame mounting.

steps.name: Stage completed Y-Left axis on bench
steps.instructionText: All Y-Left axis components are assembled — carriage, idler, motor holder, rods, and belt are complete. Pick up the assembled axis unit and place it at the staging position on your bench. It will sit here until frame mounting.

steps.name: Y-left axis bench QC and label
steps.instructionText: Run the full bench QC checklist before labeling and setting aside: belt moves smoothly along the full rod length; rod ends are flush with the idler bottom; the idler and carriage bolt heads are on the same side; the toothed side of the belt is facing inward. Label the axis Y-Left using masking tape and a marker.

steps.name: Stage completed Y-Right axis on bench
steps.instructionText: All Y-Right axis components are assembled. Pick up the completed axis unit and place it at the staging position on your bench. It sits here until frame mounting.

steps.name: Y-right axis bench QC and label
steps.instructionText: QC checklist: belt smooth along full rod length; rods flush with idler bottom; idler and carriage bolt heads same side; belt toothed side inward. Label axis Y-Right with masking tape and marker.

steps.name: Stage completed X axis on bench
steps.instructionText: The X axis is fully assembled — carriage sides, idler, motor holder, rods, belt, and endstop are complete. Place the assembled X axis at the staging position on your bench. Label it before setting down.

steps.name: X-axis bench QC, dry-fit to Y axes, and label
steps.instructionText: QC checklist: carriage moves smoothly along axis rods; rods flush with idler bottom; idler and carriage bolt heads on same side; toothed side of belt inward; belt pegs in correct holes; belt peg holes line up. Dry-fit the X-axis against the completed Y axes and confirm it will fit. Label axis X-Axis with masking tape.

steps.name: Stage completed Z-Front axis on bench
steps.instructionText: Z-Front axis assembly is complete. Place the assembled axis unit at the staging position on your bench. It will be mounted to the front face of the frame.

steps.name: Z-front axis bench QC and label
steps.instructionText: QC checklist: belt smooth along full rod length; rods flush with idler bottom; idler and carriage bolt heads on same side; toothed side of belt inward. Label axis Z-Front. Set aside for frame mounting.

steps.name: Stage completed Z-Back axis on bench
steps.instructionText: Z-Back axis assembly is complete. Place the assembled axis unit at the staging position on your bench. It mounts to the back face of the frame.

steps.name: Z-back axis bench QC and label
steps.instructionText: QC checklist: belt smooth along full rod length; rods flush with idler bottom; idler and carriage bolt heads on same side; toothed side of belt inward. Label axis Z-Back. Set aside — it mounts directly behind Z-front during frame mount.

steps.name: Tighten the Z-front idler screws
steps.instructionText: Tighten the two shorter M6x18 bolts on the Z-front idler using an electric drill on low torque. This locks the rods flush against the back of the idler. Leave the longer M6x30 bolt loose â€” it will attach to the frame.

steps.name: Mount the Z-front axis to the front frame face
steps.instructionText: Pop the loose belt peg out and set aside. Tighten all 8 motor-piece bolts before mounting (they become inaccessible after). Lengthen the axis and fit the idler long bolt through the bottom hole of the front frame face; fit the motor long bolt through the top hole. Fasten both with nuts.

steps.name: Lock the Z-front motor holder bolts
steps.instructionText: With the Z-front axis at the correct frame length, tighten the two motor-piece bolts fully. This locks the rods in place so the belt can be properly tensioned.

steps.name: Tension the Z-front axis belt
steps.instructionText: Grip the loose belt end with pliers and pull until the belt is taut enough to twang like a guitar string when plucked. Do not overtighten â€” the belt can snap. Hold tension and proceed to insert the belt peg.

steps.name: Reinsert the Z-front belt peg
steps.instructionText: Insert the belt end approximately Â¾ inch through the peg, foot facing away from the axis center. Push the peg firmly into the small ribbed belt-catcher hole to lock the belt tension.

steps.name: Check Z-front axis travel and QC
steps.instructionText: Run the Z-front QC checklist: carriage moves smoothly along full travel; rods flush with idler bottom; idler and carriage bolt heads on the same side; toothed side of belt faces inward.

steps.name: Tighten the Z-back idler screws
steps.instructionText: Tighten the two shorter M6x18 bolts on the Z-back idler using an electric drill on low torque. Leave the longer M6x30 bolt loose for frame attachment.

steps.name: Mount the Z-back axis to the back frame face
steps.instructionText: Tighten all 8 motor-piece bolts first. Lengthen the Z-back axis and fit the idler bolt through the bottom hole of the back frame face; fit the motor bolt through the top hole. Fasten both. Align directly behind Z-front.

steps.name: Lock the Z-back motor holder bolts
steps.instructionText: Tighten the two motor-piece bolts on the Z-back axis. Verify both Z columns are aligned â€” Z-back carriage should be at the same height as Z-front carriage before locking.

steps.name: Tension the Z-back axis belt
steps.instructionText: Tension the Z-back belt with pliers until it twangs like a guitar string. Hold tension and proceed to the belt peg step.

steps.name: Reinsert the Z-back belt peg
steps.instructionText: Insert belt end ~Â¾ inch through the peg, foot facing away from axis center. Push peg firmly into the ribbed belt-catcher hole.

steps.name: Check Z-back axis travel and QC
steps.instructionText: QC: carriage moves smoothly; rods flush; bolt heads same side; toothed belt faces inward; Z-back carriage level with Z-front carriage.

steps.name: Mount the bed guide rods through the Z carriages
steps.instructionText: Thread two nuts onto each bed guide rod. Slide each rod through the matching hole in the Z-front carriage and the Z-back carriage. Push rod ends flush with the outer edges of both carriages.

steps.name: Thread the bed raiser standoffs onto the guide rods
steps.instructionText: Thread three bed raiser standoffs onto each of the two guide rods. Space them evenly. The raisers create the flat support surface that the heated bed rests on and maintain the correct bed height above the carriages.

steps.name: Check bed platform alignment and rod seating
steps.instructionText: Verify: rod ends are flush with idler outer edges; two nuts per rod; bolts tightened without deforming plastic; platform does not rock laterally when pressed.

steps.name: Place Y-Axis Bed Rods
steps.instructionText: Position the Y-axis bed guide rods connecting the front and back Z carriages. These rods enable the bed platform to travel smoothly along the Y axis.

steps.name: Place Cable Management
steps.instructionText: Position the cable chain assemblies: X-axis cable chain at the top of the gantry, Y-axis cable chain along the left side, and the cable chain bracket that anchors the Y chain to the Y-left carriage.

steps.name: Mount the control panel enclosure to the frame
steps.instructionText: Align the control panel enclosure to the front-left face of the frame. Press the mounting tabs flush against the frame bars.

steps.name: Install the RAMPS 1.4 board in the control panel
steps.instructionText: Seat the RAMPS 1.4 board into the control panel. USB port and power terminals face outward. Press firmly until the board sits level.

steps.name: Secure the control panel with M3 screws
steps.instructionText: Thread M3 screws through the panel mounting tabs. Snug finger-tight, then a quarter-turn â€” do not overtighten plastic tabs.

steps.name: Connect +12V power wire from PSU to RAMPS
steps.instructionText: Connect the red +12V wire from the PSU V+ output to the V+ screw terminal on the RAMPS board. Click the red port sphere, then the RAMPS terminal sphere.

steps.name: Connect GND wire from PSU to RAMPS
steps.instructionText: Connect the black GND wire from the PSU GND output to the GND screw terminal on the RAMPS board. Polarity matters â€” do not swap with the +12V terminal.

steps.name: Short ATX PS_ON to GND (enable jumper)
steps.instructionText: Connect a short wire from the ATX PS_ON pin (green, pin 16) to a GND pin to force the PSU on. Without this jumper the printer will not power up.

steps.name: Verify all PSU connections to RAMPS
steps.instructionText: Check all three PSU connections: V+ (red wire), GND (black wire), and the PS_ON enable jumper. Gently tug each â€” a properly seated terminal will not pull free.

steps.name: Connect X-axis stepper motor to RAMPS
steps.instructionText: Plug the X-axis stepper motor 4-pin dupont connector into the X-motor header on the RAMPS board. Click the motor-side sphere, then the RAMPS header sphere.

steps.name: Connect Y-left stepper motor to RAMPS
steps.instructionText: Plug the Y1 (left) stepper motor dupont connector into the Y-motor header on RAMPS.

steps.name: Connect Y-right stepper motor to RAMPS
steps.instructionText: Plug the Y2 (right) stepper motor dupont connector into the second Y-motor header on RAMPS. Both Y motors share the Y-driver or use a splitter cable.

steps.name: Connect Z-front stepper motor to RAMPS
steps.instructionText: Plug the Z-front motor dupont connector into the Z-motor header on RAMPS. Z uses a single driver; both Z motors are wired in series or parallel via a splitter.

steps.name: Connect Z-back stepper motor to RAMPS
steps.instructionText: Plug the Z-back motor dupont connector into the Z-motor splitter or second Z header. Both Z columns must be driven together for level bed movement.

steps.name: Connect X-axis min endstop to RAMPS
steps.instructionText: Connect the X min endstop 2-pin dupont to the X-MIN header on RAMPS (S and GND pins). This is the home position limit for the X axis.

steps.name: Connect Y-axis min endstop to RAMPS
steps.instructionText: Connect the Y min endstop to the Y-MIN header on RAMPS.

steps.name: Connect Z-axis min endstop to RAMPS
steps.instructionText: Connect the Z min endstop to the Z-MIN header on RAMPS. This is triggered when the bed reaches the home position (nozzle at Z=0).

steps.name: Connect extruder heater cartridge to RAMPS
steps.instructionText: Connect the heater cartridge wires to the E0 heater output (D10) on RAMPS. Polarity does not matter for the resistive heater element.

steps.name: Connect extruder thermistor to RAMPS
steps.instructionText: Connect the thermistor to the T0 2-pin header (signal + GND). Polarity matters â€” reversing causes false 300Â°C readings and thermal runaway shutdown.

steps.name: Connect extruder part-cooling fan to RAMPS
steps.instructionText: Connect the part-cooling fan to the FAN header (always-on 12V) or D9 for PWM speed control. Red wire to +12V, black to GND.

steps.name: Route all axis cables through the cable chain
steps.instructionText: Route the X/Y motor cables and endstop wires through the cable chain. Leave 30mm of slack at each axis end for full travel. Snap the chain cover closed.

hints.title: Lower bracket placement order
hints.message: Place brackets at the four lower cube corners in diagonal order: front-left, rear-right, front-right, rear-left. Start two M6 bolts per bracket but leave them loose.
hints.text: Tabs on both bracket arms must contact the frame tube face before starting bolts. If a tab lifts, the bracket is upside-down.

hints.title: Upper bracket placement order
hints.message: Place brackets at the four upper cube corners in the same diagonal order. Start two M6 bolts per bracket but leave them loose.

hints.title: Finger-tight only
hints.message: Snug the bolts just enough to hold the bracket in place. Do not fully tighten until all 8 brackets are placed and the cube has been checked for square.
hints.text: Snug = bolt is seated and bracket cannot rock. Stop before the bracket visibly deforms. Full torque (8 Nm) comes after squaring the frame in the next steps.

hints.title: Diagonal tightening pattern
hints.message: Tighten bolts in alternating diagonal pairs to distribute clamping force evenly. For example: lower front-left, then upper rear-right, then lower front-right, then upper rear-left.

hints.title: Tack the front-left edge
hints.message: Use the MIG torch to place a short tack at the mid-height front-left edge where the front panel meets the left panel.

hints.title: Tack the front-right edge
hints.message: Use the MIG torch to place a short tack at the mid-height front-right edge where the front panel meets the right panel.

hints.title: Tack the rear-left edge
hints.message: Use the MIG torch to place a short tack at the mid-height rear-left edge where the rear panel meets the left panel.

hints.title: Tack the rear-right edge
hints.message: Use the MIG torch to place a short tack at the mid-height rear-right edge where the rear panel meets the right panel.

hints.title: Place the bottom frame side
hints.message: Place the completed bottom panel flat into the lowest cube target. This becomes the floor of the printer frame. Lower it onto the target marker and release once it seats flat.

hints.title: Place the left frame side
hints.message: Stand the left panel vertically in the left cube slot. The face with the visible overlap corner faces outward. Lower it against the bottom panel edge.

hints.title: Place the right frame side
hints.message: Stand the right panel vertically in the right cube slot, mirroring the left side. Both vertical panels should be parallel before tacking.

hints.title: Place the front frame side
hints.message: Set the front panel into the front cube slot between the left and right verticals. The panel edges butt against the left and right panel faces.

hints.title: Place the rear frame side
hints.message: Set the rear panel into the rear cube slot mirroring the front panel. The cube is now fully enclosed on five faces.

hints.title: Place the top frame side
hints.message: Lay the top panel flat onto the top of the stacked cube to close all six faces. The full cube layout is now ready for the stacked-cube square check.

hints.title: Angle grinder technique on corner seams
hints.message: Hold the grinder disc at a shallow angle and use short back-and-forth passes. Remove spatter and proud bead so the upper bracket will sit fully flush. Wear face shield and leather gloves.

hints.title: Final frame acceptance check
hints.message: Measure both diagonals of the top face one last time. Both readings should match within 1 mm. Confirm only when the frame is square and the brackets are fully tight.

hints.title: Seat Y-left idler through top-right frame hole
hints.message: Loosen the long bolt on the idler piece first. Place the idler onto the left face of the frame so the long bolt goes through the top-right hole. Attach a nut to the back of that bolt and fasten with drill. Then push the motor holder outward to span the frame, tighten all 8 motor-holder bolts before anchoring (they become inaccessible once the axis is mounted). Fasten the motor-holder long bolt to the frame and tighten each nut with pliers.

hints.title: Carriage shake test + rod-slide test
hints.message: Run both bearing fit tests before calling the axis good. Shake test: compress the carriage halves by hand and shake â€” bearings must not rattle inside. Rod-slide test: hold the carriage with a rod running vertically â€” the rod should slide through with slight resistance, not drop freely. If too loose, wrap each bearing in one layer of electrical tape; if too tight, remove tape. Also push the carriage along the full rod length â€” no binding.

hints.title: Seat Y-right idler and lock all 8 motor-holder bolts
hints.message: Mirror the Y-left procedure for the right face. Loosen the idler long bolt, fit it through the correct frame hole, and secure with a nut. Span the axis outward until the motor-holder long bolt reaches the opposite frame hole. Tighten all 8 motor-holder bolts (3 M6x18 + 4 M3 screws + long M6x30) before fastening to frame â€” they are inaccessible afterward. Anchor both ends with pliers.

hints.title: Both Y axes: smooth motion, end stop, motors at back
hints.message: Check all four items: (1) Carriage slides smoothly along each Y axis without binding. (2) Each motor shaft spins freely when you turn the carriage by hand. (3) On Y-left, push the carriage into the end stop and confirm it clicks. (4) Both motors are at the back of the frame â€” the axes are mirror images of each other.

hints.title: Tighten the two shorter M6x18 idler bolts; leave M6x30 loose
hints.message: Use a drill on low torque to tighten the two shorter M6x18 bolts on the idler. These lock the rods flush against the back of the idler so they cannot be pushed out when the axis is extended. Leave the longer M6x30 bolt loose â€” it will attach the idler to the Y-right carriage in the next step.

hints.title: Drop two M6x30 bolts through Y-right carriage into idler
hints.message: Insert two M6x30 bolts through the Y-right carriage from the outside. Move the X-axis so those bolts enter the idler nut catchers. Fasten with drill on low-torque setting â€” do not overtighten. The idler is now anchored; the motor-holder side remains free to slide outward for the span-fit step. Note: it does not matter yet whether the X-axis is perfectly vertical.

hints.title: Snug motor-holder bolts then back off half a turn each
hints.message: Before extending the X-axis to its final span, minimize play in the motor holder: tighten the 3 M6x18 bolts and 4 M3 motor screws by hand until snug, then loosen each by half a turn. The long M6x30 bolt stays completely loose at this stage. The holder must still slide freely along the rods for the span-fit step â€” but without excessive wobble.

hints.title: Finger-start motor-holder bolts only, no tightening
hints.message: Thread the two Y-left M6x30 bolts in by hand only, two to three turns each. Do not tighten. The motor-holder must still slide freely along the X rods for the span-fit step.

hints.title: Push Y carriages to rod ends; extend X to Y-left face
hints.message: For the most accurate span fit, slide both Y-axis carriages toward the far ends of the Y rods where the rods are most constrained. Then push the X-axis motor holder outward until it butts flush against the Y-left carriage face. While extending, regularly check that both rods remain flush in the idler â€” if they start to protrude, push them back in.

hints.title: Loose-attach Y-left bolts, adjust vertical, then fully lock
hints.message: Insert two M6x30 bolts through the Y-left carriage into the motor-holder nut catchers. Hand-tighten with allen key just enough to support the axis. Adjust the X-axis to vertical before the final tighten. Once vertical, use a drill to fasten both Y-left M6x30 bolts. Then tighten the 3 M6x18 and 4 M3 motor-holder screws fully. All 8 motor-holder fasteners must be locked before tensioning the belt.

hints.title: Pull belt taut with pliers â€” twang test before inserting peg
hints.message: Grip the loose belt end with pliers and pull, or grip and twist, until the belt is taut. Pluck the belt â€” it should twang like a guitar string. Be firm but careful: excessive force can snap the belt. When the belt feels correct, hold tension and insert the belt peg in the next step without releasing.

hints.title: Insert belt ~Â¾" through peg foot; foot faces away from center
hints.message: Thread the belt end through the inside of the peg approximately Â¾ inch, with the peg foot turned away from the toothed side of the belt. Quick check: the foot faces away from the center of the axis assembly; the belt passes through the carriage before going through the foot. Insert the peg into the small, ribbed belt-catcher hole and push firmly. This peg locks the belt tail so tension is maintained.

hints.title: Four-point X-axis QC: travel, flush rods, bolt sides, belt direction
hints.message: Run the full QC checklist before calling the X-axis complete: (1) Carriage moves smoothly along full X travel â€” no binding. Lubricate rods if tightness remains after checking rod alignment. (2) Rods are flush with the idler bottom â€” not protruding. (3) Idler and carriage bolt heads are on the same side. (4) Toothed side of belt faces inward. (5) X-axis butts tightly to both Y-axes with no visible gap. (6) Extruder carriage reaches and triggers the X-axis end stop.

hints.title: Orient Titan Aero with nozzle port accessible from below
hints.message: Place the Titan Aero core with the motor and drive face pointing outward and the nozzle port accessible from below. This lets the blower and sensor-side parts attach without reorienting the module.

hints.title: Seat nozzle assembly square to core face
hints.message: Push the nozzle assembly onto the Titan Aero core until it seats fully and sits square to the core face. Any tilt here will carry through to fan and sensor alignment.

hints.title: Blower outlet faces toward nozzle tip
hints.message: Place the 5015 blower onto the fan-side attachment point with the outlet directed toward the nozzle tip. Seat the body fully before tightening the mount screws.

hints.title: Small screwdriver for blower mount screws
hints.message: Tighten both blower mount screws with the small screwdriver until snug. The blower body should not rock or shift when pushed sideways.

hints.title: Sensor holder mounts opposite the blower
hints.message: Place the printed sensor holder on the sensor-side mount point of the nozzle module on the opposite face from the blower.

hints.title: Lock holder before inserting sensor
hints.message: Tighten both holder fasteners with the 2.5 mm hex driver before inserting the 8 mm sensor. A loose holder lets the sensor shift during printing and causes inconsistent bed leveling.

hints.title: Insert sensor flush with holder bottom
hints.message: Slide the 8 mm sensor into the holder until its tip sits flush with or slightly recessed below the holder bottom. The sensor face should be near-level with the nozzle tip plane.

hints.title: Inspect nozzle, fan, and sensor clearance
hints.message: Check each of the three sides in turn. Nozzle tip must be exposed, blower outlet must not be blocked, sensor face must be clear. Each side should have a few millimetres of working space.

hints.title: Set carriage with bracket-mount face up
hints.message: Place the simplified carriage flat with the bracket-mounting face pointing upward. This face receives the Titan Aero mount bracket in the next step.

hints.title: Bracket aligns to carriage fastener holes
hints.message: Set the Titan Aero mount bracket against the carriage face and align the two fastener holes. The bracket should drop flat with light hand pressure. Do not force.

hints.title: Tighten bracket bolts evenly side to side
hints.message: Tighten the two bracket fasteners alternately, a quarter turn each side, so the bracket pulls down evenly. A tilted bracket will offset the nozzle from the carriage axis.

hints.title: Top plate captures the bracket from above
hints.message: Place the top plate over the bracket with both fastener holes aligned. The top plate locks the bracket against the carriage surface when its two fasteners are tightened.

hints.title: Fully tighten top-plate fasteners before mounting module
hints.message: Tighten both top-plate fasteners fully with the 2.5 mm hex driver. The entire bracket stack must be locked solid before the completed nozzle module is mounted onto it.

hints.title: Keep nozzle module square when seating on bracket stack
hints.message: Bring the completed Titan Aero nozzle module onto the bracket stack square and flat. The module rigid-mounts through the bracket. No fastener rotation is needed, just correct alignment.

hints.title: Confirm service access on all three extruder sides
hints.message: Check the extruder body, nozzle exit, and sensor side in turn. Each must have enough open space that a technician could remove the sensor or nozzle after the carriage is fully integrated.

hints.title: Carriage side in work zone with rod axis horizontal
hints.message: Place the X-axis carriage-side interface with its rod-hole axis running horizontally. The completed extruder carriage unit will mount onto the front face in the next step.

hints.title: Lower extruder carriage unit onto interface face
hints.message: Lower the completed extruder carriage unit onto the X-axis carriage-side interface. The two bodies align through the bracket mount face. If it does not seat cleanly, check orientation before applying force.

hints.title: Half carriage clamps from the opposite side
hints.message: Bring the half carriage in square against the rod path on the back side of the interface. The two body halves should close together around the X rods with no gap on either side.

hints.title: Check both travel limits and nozzle tip clearance
hints.message: Move the extruder carriage to the left limit and right limit by hand with no interference. Then confirm the nozzle tip clears the frame rails and future bed area at all positions.

hints.title: Center bed body under nozzle travel path
hints.message: Place the heatbed body roughly centered under the nozzle travel area. The two snapbuckle retainers will hold it in position in the next two steps.

hints.title: Left snapbuckle clicks into left bed edge
hints.message: Press the left snapbuckle firmly onto the left side of the bed body until it clicks into the retainer channel. The fit should be firm with no visible gap.

hints.title: Right snapbuckle mirrors the left
hints.message: Press the right snapbuckle onto the right side of the bed body until it clicks. The bed should now be retained symmetrically on both sides with equal pressure.

hints.title: Check bed centering and nozzle-to-bed clearance
hints.message: Inspect both bed-to-frame margins. They should be roughly equal. Move the nozzle carriage across the full range and confirm the nozzle tip stays above the bed surface at every position.

hints.title: Stage 1 mechanical assembly complete
hints.message: All mechanical components for the D3D Stage 1 assembly are in place. Rotate the view to inspect the full printer before advancing to the internal axis detail stages.

hints.title: Shake test + rod-slide test before bolting
hints.message: Run both tests before tightening. Shake test: close halves by hand and shake — bearings must not rattle. Rod-slide test: hold carriage vertically with rod through it — rod should drop with slight resistance, not freely. One layer of electrical tape per bearing if too loose. Belt-hole alignment: small ribbed hole and large smooth hole must match — the belt peg goes into the small ribbed hole.

hints.title: Rod holes must align; frame-mount bolt stays loose
hints.message: When closing the two idler halves, confirm the circular rod holes on each half line up — misaligned rod holes will bind the rods. The long M6x30 bolt is the frame-mount bolt; leave it and the last M6x18 bolt both very loose. They will only be tightened after the axis is sized to fit the frame.

hints.title: Pulley pop = fully seated; cable plug faces belt side
hints.message: Tighten the set screw until you hear a pop — that is the set screw slipping on the motor shaft at maximum engagement. It is OK if you do not hear a pop; just tighten as hard as you can. Motor orientation: the cable plug must face toward the bottom-right (toward the belt drive hole) so the wire wraps cleanly later. The M6x30 bolt (the long one) pokes UP from the motor half-piece — put nuts in the other three holes before closing.

hints.title: Rods flush at idler; carriage long bolts toward idler
hints.message: Rod ends must be flush against the bottom of the idler — if they stick out the back, loosen the idler bolts slightly, re-seat the rods, then re-tighten. Carriage goes on with its long bolt ends closer to the idler — this maximizes travel range. Motor holder goes on with the motor facing the carriage bolt heads. After the motor holder is on, re-check that rods are still flush at the idler.

hints.title: Curve the belt before threading around the idler bearing
hints.message: Give the belt a slight pre-curve in the correct direction before sliding it inside the idler — it will seat around the bearing much more easily. The foot of the peg must face away from the center of the axis. Catch the belt end approximately ¾ inch under the peg before pushing the peg in. First peg gets pushed in firmly. Second peg is placed loosely — it will be tightened with pliers after the axis is mounted on the frame and the full belt length is confirmed.

hints.title: Switch arm faces arrowhead; no glue on metal switch
hints.message: The endstop board should slide into the crevice with the switch arm pointing in the arrowhead direction the holder piece makes — away from the mounting legs. The bottom of the board should be flush with the bottom of the holder. Do not put super glue on the metal switch itself — only in the crevice and along the outer edges of the holder-to-motor-piece joint.

hints.title: Four-point bench QC before labeling
hints.message: Check all four before calling it done: (1) Belt moves smoothly along full rod travel — no binding. (2) Rod ends flush with idler bottom — push them back if sticking out. (3) Idler bolt heads and carriage bolt heads on the same side. (4) Toothed side of belt faces inward toward the motor pulley. Label Y-Left with masking tape and marker. Set aside for frame mounting.

hints.title: Same carriage build as Y-left
hints.message: Identical procedure to Y-left carriage: four bearings flanges-out, shake test, rod-slide test, tape if loose, align belt holes, bolt halves.

hints.title: Orient with large rectangular hole to the left
hints.message: For Y-right idler: orient with large rectangular hole facing left. M6x30 screw inserts into the top hole with screw end pointing toward you. It doesn't matter which direction you face the other bolts — but make sure all future bolts face the same way. Leave frame-mount bolts very loose.

hints.title: Long M6x30 bolt in bottom-left hole; cable plug faces belt drive
hints.message: Y-right motor holder key differences: the M6x30 bolt goes in the BOTTOM-LEFT hole (the right side of the half-piece has the belt drive). The motor cable plug faces the belt drive hole, which is opposite to where the long M6x30 bolt is — this allows wires to wrap neatly during final assembly.

hints.title: Same rod threading as Y-left
hints.message: Same sequence: rods into idler flush, carriage long bolts toward idler, motor faces carriage bolt heads. Tighten idler two shorter bolts. Re-check rods flush.

hints.title: Belt through larger hole, around idler via catcher side
hints.message: Thread belt through larger carriage hole, around idler bearing via the belt-catcher side (smaller hole with teeth on one side). Lock first peg firmly. Place second peg loosely. Label Y-Right.

hints.title: Four-point bench QC and label Y-Right
hints.message: Same four-point QC as Y-left. Label axis Y-Right. The two Y axes should be mirror images of each other when placed side by side — motors at back, carriages toward center.

hints.title: X carriage middle holes fit as shown
hints.message: X-axis carriage is the same bearing-in-carriage build. The middle holes must fit together as pictured — this aligns the extruder mount holes. Run shake and rod-slide tests.

hints.title: X idler: half-bearings + nuts for Y-carriage attachment
hints.message: X-axis idler uses 2 half-bearings (not round bearings) and 5 M6x18 screws. Place bearings with flanges facing out. The nuts placed beside the bearings in their slots are critical — these are the attachment points where the Y-carriage M6x30 bolts thread in to anchor the X-axis. Close and tighten the idler fully.

hints.title: Two nuts in half-piece first; cable plug toward belt
hints.message: X motor holder: set two nuts in the motor half-piece first. Belt toothed-side inward in channel. Insert 4 nuts (no bolts yet) when closing halves. Motor cable plug points toward belt. Note the exact position of the long M6x30 bolt in relation to the belt hole — it must be on the correct side for the belt to route properly.

hints.title: Motor holder orientation is critical for X-axis
hints.message: When pushing the motor holder onto the X-axis rods, orientation matters — check the picture carefully. The motor holder must face a specific direction relative to the idler for the belt to route correctly and the carriage to travel in the right direction.

hints.title: Second peg stays loose until fitted between Y axes
hints.message: Route same as Y axes: large carriage hole → around idler bearing → first peg locked firmly. The second peg is placed loosely and will be tightened only AFTER the X-axis is fitted and the span is locked between the Y-axis carriages.

hints.title: X endstop glues same way as Y-left endstop
hints.message: Same gluing procedure as Y-left endstop. Dry-fit the completed X-axis against the Y axes before setting aside — confirm the idler nuts align with the Y-right carriage holes.

hints.title: Six-point QC + dry-fit to Y axes
hints.message: X-axis QC has six points: carriage slides smoothly; rods flush at idler; bolt heads same side; belt toothed side inward; belt pegs in correct holes; belt peg holes line up. Also dry-fit the X-axis against both Y-axis carriages and confirm it will span correctly before labeling.

hints.title: Z-Front uses same pieces as Y-Right
hints.message: Z-Front uses the identical plastic piece type as Y-Right. Same carriage build procedure. The axis module is functionally the same — it is only mounted in a different orientation (vertical on front frame face instead of horizontal on side face).

hints.title: Frame-mount bolt to bottom-right hole of front face
hints.message: Z-front idler long bolt goes through the bottom hole on the front face of the frame. The axis is positioned to the right of the hole center — this offset maximizes the available print area. Leave the long bolt very loose at bench.

hints.title: Pre-tighten all 8 motor-piece bolts at bench
hints.message: Critical for Z axes: tighten all 8 motor-piece bolts (3 M6x18 + 4 M3x25 + 1 M6x30) to final torque at bench — they become inaccessible once the axis is mounted and sized to the frame. This is called out in the Final Assembly PDF as a mandatory pre-tighten step.

hints.title: Spacers go between carriage and half-carriage
hints.message: Z-front has two spacers (z2_spacer and z2_spacer_2) that go between the carriage half and the half-carriage piece during rod threading. These maintain the correct spacing for Z-axis travel.

hints.title: No endstop on Z axes
hints.message: Z axes do not have endstop sensors. Route and peg the belt same as Y axes. Second peg left loose for frame-side tensioning.

hints.title: Four-point QC and label Z-Front
hints.message: Same four-point QC as Y axes. Label Z-Front. Set aside — it mounts on the front frame face.

hints.title: Z-Back uses same pieces as Y-Left
hints.message: Z-Back uses the identical plastic piece type as Y-Left. Same carriage build procedure.

hints.title: Z-Back mounts directly behind Z-Front
hints.message: Z-back idler long bolt goes through the bottom hole on the back face. During frame mounting, verify Z-back is aligned directly behind Z-front before locking. Both Z carriages must be at the same height when the motor-piece bolts are locked.

hints.title: Pre-tighten all 8 motor-piece bolts at bench
hints.message: Same as Z-front: pre-tighten all 8 motor-piece bolts at bench before mounting. They become inaccessible after mounting on the back frame face.

hints.title: Spacers z1_spacer_1 and z1_spacer_002
hints.message: Z-back spacers are z1_spacer_1 and z1_spacer_002 — place between carriage and half-carriage same as Z-front.

hints.title: No endstop; second peg loose for frame tensioning
hints.message: No endstop on Z-back. Route and lock first peg. Second peg loose.

hints.title: Four-point QC and label Z-Back
hints.message: Same four-point QC. Label Z-Back. Set aside — it mounts on the back frame face directly behind Z-front.

hints.title: Z-axis front column components
hints.message: The front Z column raises and lowers the X gantry on the front side. It contains a NEMA 17 stepper motor, motor mount bracket, half carriage, lower leadscrew idler, and two column spacers.

hints.title: Z-axis back column components
hints.message: The back Z column mirrors the front. Both columns move in sync to keep the X gantry level as it travels up and down the leadscrews, setting the print layer height.

hints.title: Bed platform: raisers and guide rods
hints.message: Six standoff raisers set the heated bed at the correct height above the Z carriages. Two horizontal bed guide rods stabilize the platform side to side. Together they form the mechanical bed support structure.

hints.title: Y-axis bed guide rods
hints.message: Three rods run front-to-back connecting the front and back Z carriages. The bed platform slides along these rods as the printer moves the bed on the Y axis during a print.

hints.title: Cable chain routing and bracket
hints.message: The X-axis cable chain protects carriage wiring as it moves left and right. The Y-axis cable chain protects bed wiring as it moves front and back. The bracket mounts the X chain origin to the frame.

hints.title: Tighten the two shorter M6x18 bolts; leave M6x30 loose
hints.message: Use a drill on low torque to tighten the two shorter M6x18 bolts on the Z-front idler. These lock the rods flush against the back of the idler. Leave the longer M6x30 bolt loose â€” it attaches to the bottom of the front frame face in the next step.

hints.title: Idler to frame bottom; motor to frame top â€” tighten all 8 motor bolts first
hints.message: Pop out the belt peg that does NOT have a belt passing through it and set it aside. Tighten all 8 motor-piece bolts (3 M6x18 + 4 M3 screws + 1 M6x30) before mounting to frame â€” they become inaccessible once the axis is on the frame. Fit the long idler bolt through the bottom hole of the front frame face and fasten with a nut. Lengthen the axis until the motor-holder long bolt reaches the top frame hole and fasten. While extending, keep checking that rods stay flush in the idler.

hints.title: Lock motor-piece bolts to set the span
hints.message: Tighten both motor-piece bolts fully once the axis is at the correct frame length. These prevent the rods from shifting during belt tensioning. Use the allen key to reach the bolts â€” they are on the face that was accessible before mounting.

hints.title: Pliers tension â€” belt should twang like a guitar string
hints.message: Grip the loose belt end with pliers and pull, or grip and twist, until the belt is taut. Pluck the belt â€” it should twang like a guitar string. Be firm but careful: excessive force can snap the belt. Hold tension while reinserting the belt peg in the next step.

hints.title: Insert belt ~Â¾" through peg; foot faces away from axis center
hints.message: Thread the belt end approximately Â¾ inch through the peg, with the peg foot turned away from the toothed side of the belt. The foot faces away from the center of the axis assembly. Push the peg firmly into the small, ribbed belt-catcher hole to lock belt tension.

hints.title: Four-point Z-front QC: travel, rods flush, bolt sides, belt direction
hints.message: Run the full QC checklist: (1) Carriage moves smoothly along full Z travel â€” no binding; lubricate rods if tight. (2) Rods are flush with the idler bottom â€” not protruding. (3) Idler and carriage bolt heads are on the same side. (4) Toothed side of belt faces inward.

hints.title: Tighten the two shorter M6x18 bolts on Z-back; leave M6x30 loose
hints.message: Use a drill on low torque to tighten the two shorter M6x18 bolts on the Z-back idler. These lock the rods flush against the back of the idler. Leave the longer M6x30 bolt loose â€” it attaches to the bottom of the back frame face in the next step.

hints.title: Z-back mirrors Z-front â€” idler to back-face bottom, motor to top
hints.message: This step is identical to Z-front but mounts onto the back face of the frame. Tighten all 8 motor-piece bolts before mounting. Fit idler long bolt to bottom back-face hole, motor long bolt to top back-face hole. Keep checking rods stay flush while lengthening. The Z-back axis should align directly behind the Z-front axis.

hints.title: Lock Z-back motor-piece bolts
hints.message: Tighten both motor-piece bolts on the Z-back axis once it is at the correct frame length. The two Z columns must be aligned â€” check that the Z-back axis sits directly behind the Z-front axis before locking.

hints.title: Tension Z-back belt to guitar-string tautness
hints.message: Grip the loose belt end with pliers and pull until taut. Pluck the belt â€” guitar-string twang is the target. Hold tension and insert the belt peg in the next step without releasing.

hints.title: Insert Z-back belt peg ~Â¾"; foot away from axis center
hints.message: Thread the belt end approximately Â¾ inch through the peg foot, with the foot turned away from the center of the axis assembly. Push the peg firmly into the small, ribbed belt-catcher hole.

hints.title: Z-back QC: travel, rods flush, bolt sides, belt direction
hints.message: QC checklist: (1) Carriage moves smoothly along full Z travel. (2) Rods flush with idler bottom. (3) Idler and carriage bolt heads on the same side. (4) Toothed side of belt faces inward. (5) Z-back carriage is at the same height as Z-front carriage â€” they must stay level to keep the bed flat.

hints.title: Slide both bed rods through front and back Z carriages
hints.message: Each bed guide rod threads through the hole in the Z-front carriage and the matching hole in the Z-back carriage. Thread two nuts onto each rod before inserting â€” the nuts sit on either side of the carriage to provide a flat support surface for the heated bed and to minimize heat transfer to the plastic. Push the rod ends flush with the outer edges of both carriages.

hints.title: Thread 3 standoff raisers onto each rod; space them evenly
hints.message: Thread the 6 bed raiser standoffs onto the two guide rods â€” 3 per rod. Space them evenly along the rod length so the heated bed will sit flat. The raisers provide the correct height gap between the Z carriage and the heated bed and allow wiring to pass freely beneath the bed surface.

hints.title: Confirm rods are flush, raisers level, and no side-to-side rock
hints.message: QC checklist: (1) Rod ends are flush with the outer edges of both idler pieces â€” not protruding. (2) Two nuts are present on each rod. (3) Bolts are tightened enough to hold the rods but not so tight they deform the plastic. (4) The platform does not rock side to side when you apply light lateral pressure.
Align the control panel enclosure to the front-left face of the frame. The mounting tabs should sit flush against the frame bars before fastening.
Seat the RAMPS 1.4 board firmly into the control panel. The USB port and power screw terminals should face outward. Ensure no pins are bent when pressing the board down.
Thread M3 screws through the panel mounting tabs. Snug finger-tight first, then apply a quarter-turn with a screwdriver. Do not overtighten plastic tabs.
The +12V line (red wire) connects to the V+ screw terminal on RAMPS. Strip 6mm of insulation, insert the bare end, and tighten the terminal screw until the wire does not pull free.
The ground line (black wire) connects to the GND screw terminal adjacent to V+. Polarity matters â€” reversing +12V and GND will damage the RAMPS board immediately.
Short the PS_ON pin (green wire) to a GND pin on the ATX connector to force the PSU on. Without this jumper the PSU will not supply power when switched on.
Check all three PSU connections: V+ (red), GND (black), and the PS_ON enable jumper. Wiggle each wire gently â€” a properly seated terminal will not move.
Stepper motor connectors are 4-pin dupont. Orientation matters for motor direction but not for damage â€” if the axis runs backwards in firmware, reverse the connector.
Endstop connectors are 2-pin dupont (signal + GND). Connect to the MIN port for the corresponding axis on RAMPS (X-MIN, Y-MIN, Z-MIN). Do not use the MAX ports for home endstops.
The heater cartridge wires (red/white pair) connect to the E0 heater output on RAMPS (D10 terminal). Polarity does not matter for a resistive heater element.
The thermistor connects to the T0 pin header (2-pin, signal + GND). Polarity matters â€” reversing the thermistor will read ambient temperature as 300Â°C and trigger thermal runaway protection.
The part-cooling fan connects to the always-on 12V fan header (pins labelled FAN). If using a controlled fan, use the D9 PWM output instead.
Route all axis motor cables through the cable chain before securing the chain to the frame. Leave 30mm of slack at each axis end to allow full travel without tension.
